Ayah

الٓر ۚ تِلْكَ ءَايَـٰتُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ (۱)
Alif, Lam, Ra. These are the signs of the Wise Book. (1)

Key Points

| Six consecutive Surahs begin with the disconnected letters (Ḥurūf Muqaṭṭaʿāt) "الر": Yunus, Hud, Yusuf, Ra'd, Ibrahim, and Hijr. However, in Surah Ra'd, "المر" appears. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said regarding the disconnected letters "الر": (Alif) symbolizes Anā (I), (Lām) symbolizes Allāh, and (Rā’) symbolizes Ra’ūf (Compassionate); meaning Anā Allāh al-Ra’ūf (I am Allah, the Compassionate). (196) And in another narration, we read that these letters are among the ambiguous verses (Mutashābihāt) of the Quran. (197)

196) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 197) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The Quran holds a sublime status. "تلك" (That) refers to something distant (and elevated).

2- The Quran is the Wise Book (al-Kitāb al-Ḥakīm); it is both a judge and an arbiter, it contains wise content, and it possesses a firm structure. (History, the passage of time, and enemies cannot introduce any flaw into the Quran.) "الكتاب الحكیم"

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah Yunus The tenth Surah of the Holy Quran, which was revealed in Mecca during the early period of the Prophetic mission, is named "Yunus." This Surah has 109 verses, and its main topics revolve around Monotheism (Tawhid) and the truthfulness of the Quran, responding to the deniers of revelation, warning the polytheists, explaining the greatness of creation and the Creator, the instability of the world, and drawing attention to the Hereafter. The previous Surah, namely At-Tawbah, discussed the behavior of the hypocrites and their punishment; this Surah addresses the behavior of the polytheists.

Ayah

أَكَانَ لِلنَّاسِ عَجَبًا أَنْ أَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ رَجُلٍ مِّنْهُمْ أَنْ أَنذِرِ ٱلنَّاسَ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ أَنَّ لَهُمْ قَدَمَ صِدْقٍ عِندَ رَبِّهِمْ ۗ قَالَ ٱلْكَـٰفِرُونَ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَسَـٰحِرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۲)
Does it seem odd to these people that We have revealed to a man from among themselves, [declaring], ‘Warn mankind, and give good news to the faithful that they are in good standing with their Lord’? The faithless say, ‘This is indeed a plain magician.’ (2)

Key Points

«قَدم صِدق» (Qadam Sidq - True Standing) has several meanings: 1. Good precedence. Such as «قدمٌ فى الحرب، قدم فى الاسلام» (precedence in war, precedence in Islam), meaning he has a record in struggle and in Islam. 2. The station and rank of truthfulness and goodness. 3. The leader and guide of truth. 4. In Shi'a and Sunni narrations, «قَدَم صِدق» is identified as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله and Ali عليه السلام. (198) 5. Intercession. In a narration, the meaning of «قدم صدق» is introduced as the station of intercession. (199)

The disbelievers had no reason or logic in rejecting the Prophet's mission; rather, by deeming it improbable, they shirked accepting it. Just as they denied the fundamental principles of faith merely with astonishment: Regarding Monotheism: «اَجَعل الآلهة الهاً واحداً» (200) Regarding Prophethood: «اهذا الّذى بعث اللَّه رسولاً» (201) Regarding Resurrection: «من یحیى العظام و هى رمیم» (202) Regarding Imamate: «انّى یكون له الملك ولم یؤت سعةً من المال» (203)

198) Tafasir Qurtubi and Burhan. 199) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 200) Sad, 5. 201) Furqan, 41. 202) Yasin, 78. 203) Baqarah, 247.

Messages

  1. The root of disbelief is often deeming revelation improbable and astonishing. «عجبا ان اوحینا»
  2. The source of the prophets' call is Divine revelation. «اوحینا الى رجل»
  3. The spiritual merits of individuals are not visible outwardly. If someone receives Allah's special favor, we should accept him. «رجل منهم»
  4. Prophets, because they are role models, must be from the people and familiar with their pains. «منهم»
  5. The duty of the prophets is to give glad tidings and warnings. «اَنذر، بشّر»
  6. We should not reject everything we do not understand, because denial and accusation are the methods of the disbelievers. «قال الكافرونَ انّ هذا لساحِرٌ مُبین»
  7. Faith is the basis for a true standing (Qadam Sidq) and a special status with Allah. «انّ لهم الجنّة»

Ayah

إِنَّ رَبَّكُمُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِى خَلَقَ ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضَ فِى سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ثُمَّ ٱسْتَوَىٰ عَلَى ٱلْعَرْشِ ۖ يُدَبِّرُ ٱلْأَمْرَ ۖ مَا مِن شَفِيعٍ إِلَّا مِنۢ بَعْدِ إِذْنِهِۦ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمْ فَٱعْبُدُوهُ ۚ أَفَلَا تَذَكَّرُونَ (۳)
Indeed your Lord is Allah, who created the heavens and the earth in six days, and then settled on the Throne, directing the command. There is no intercessor, except by His leave. That is Allah, your Lord! So worship Him. Will you not then take admonition? (3)

Key Points

The meaning of "six days" is six ages, six stages, and six eras in creation, and it is possible that it refers to a duration equal to six days. The 'Arsh (Throne) is the center of administration and a metaphor for power. When they say: so-and-so sat on the throne, or they pulled him down from the throne, it means he attained power or was deposed. Allah's sovereignty existed over creation both before the creation of the heavens and the earth, «وكان عرشه على الماء» (204), and after their creation, and His divine sovereignty over all existence remains even after the end of the world and on the Day of Judgment. «یحمل عرش ربّك یومئذٍ ثمانیة» (205) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The Throne ('Arsh) is square, because the words upon which the structure of Islam is built are also four words: «سبحان اللّه والحمدللّه ولااله الااللّه واللّه اكبر». (206)

204) Hud, 7. 205) Al-Haqqah, 17 206) Ilal al-Shara'i', p. 398.

Messages

1- The creation of the universe took place with planning and scheduling. «ستّة ایّام» (When planning and administration govern the system of existence, how can it be accepted that man, who is the crown jewel of creation, should be without a plan?!)

2- First knowledge of Allah, then worship of Allah. «ان ربكم اللَّه الذى ... فاعبدوه»

3- Existence is governed by law and has a purpose. The necessity of administering the affair is having an outcome, a goal, and being governed by law. «یدبّر الامر»

4- Worship is worthy of Him in whose hand is creation and administration, not others. «خلق... یدبّر... ذلكم اللَّه ربّكم فاعبدوه»

5- Allah created existence, «خَلق», and holds it in His grasp, «استوى», and constantly manages it with wisdom, «یدّبر», and no one has influence without His permission, «مامن شفیع». Attention to these realities is the foundation for the emergence of the spirit of servitude and worship. «فاعبدوه»

6- The intercession of any being must be confirmed by Allah. Therefore, we should not appoint idols as intercessors without reason. «ما من شفیع الاّ من بعد باذنه»

7- Divinity (Ulūhiyyah) is not separate from Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). Tyrants (Tāghūts) try to acknowledge Allah as the Creator but claim themselves as the policymaker and administrator, and they try to separate religion from politics. «اللَّه ربّكم»

8- Man believes in his Creator; he only needs reminder. «افلاتذكّرون»

Ayah

إِلَيْهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ جَمِيعًا ۖ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقًّا ۚ إِنَّهُۥ يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ لِيَجْزِىَ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۚ وَٱلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا۟ لَهُمْ شَرَابٌ مِّنْ حَمِيمٍ وَعَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ (٤)
To Him will be the return of you all; [that is] Allah’s true promise. Indeed, He originates the creation, then He will bring it back that He may reward with justice those who have faith and do righteous deeds. As for the faithless, they shall have boiling water for drink, and a painful punishment because of what they used to defy. (4)

Key Points

This Ayah addresses the principle of the Resurrection (Ma'ad), «الیه مرجعكم» (to Him is your return), its possibility, «یبدؤا الخلق ثم یعیده» (He originates the creation, then He repeats it), and the goal of the Resurrection, which is reward and punishment, «لیجزى» (that He may recompense). It is probable that the intent of «بالقسط» (with justice) is that the people of faith receive their reward because of the justice (qist) and equity they possess. [207] Of course, Divine grace (fadl) does not contradict justice (qist), just as other verses refer to additional Divine rewards, including: «للذین احسنوا الحسنى و زیادة» [208] and «لیوفیّهم اجورهم ویزیدهم من فضله» [209]

207) Tafsirs Majma' al-Bayan and Kabir Fakhr Razi. 208) Yunus, 26. 209) Fatir, 30.

Messages

1- Our return is to Allah; let us not waste time thinking about satisfying this person or that one. «الیه مرجعكم»

2- All human beings will be resurrected after death. «الیه مرجعكم جمیعاً»

3- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) is material and physical. «یبدء الخلق ثم یعیده» (Reiteration [i'adah] means returning the very same thing.)

4- Allah’s ability to originate creation is a sign of His ability to resurrect. «یبدء الخلق ثم یعیده»

5- Not only will man be resurrected, but the entire creation will be created anew. «یبدء الخلق ثم یعیده»

6- Just as the entire existence was created for mankind, the reiteration of creation in the Hereafter is also for mankind to receive reward. «یبدء الخلق ثم یعیده لیجزى...»

7- Creation continues to be sustained. «یبدء الخلق»

8- The primary purpose of the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is the reward of the believers. «لیجزى الذین امنوا» And the punishment of the disbelievers is due to Divine justice.

9- Divine reward is based on equity (qist) and is just. (This does not contradict the increase granted through His grace.) «لیجزیهم... بالقسط»

10- The value of good deeds lies in their good motivation and goal. «آمنوا و عملوا الصالحات»

11- Persistence and insistence upon disbelief lead to punishment. «بما كانوا یكفرون»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ ٱلشَّمْسَ ضِيَآءً وَٱلْقَمَرَ نُورًا وَقَدَّرَهُۥ مَنَازِلَ لِتَعْلَمُوا۟ عَدَدَ ٱلسِّنِينَ وَٱلْحِسَابَ ۚ مَا خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ ذَٰلِكَ إِلَّا بِٱلْحَقِّ ۚ يُفَصِّلُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَعْلَمُونَ (٥)
It is He who made the sun a radiance and the moon a light, and ordained its phases that you might know the number of years and the calculation [of time]. Allah did not create all that except with justice. He elaborates the signs for a people who have knowledge. (5)

Key Points

In the Quran, words like sirāj (سراج) and ḍiyā’ (ضیاء) are used for the sun, which mean intense and powerful light. Regarding the moon, the words nūr (نور) and munīr (مُنیر) are used, which also include weak light. However, if we consider ḍiyā’ (ضیاء) to be the plural of ḍaw’ (ضوء), it might indicate that the sun possesses various kinds of light. [210] The intent of tafṣīl al-āyāt (تفصیل آیات) is perhaps the expansion in creation, meaning that Allah’s Hand is open in the creation of existence, and the expansion of creation is clear for those who understand. [211] Just as the Wise Allah has placed markers in the heavens for counting the years, He has also placed messengers and guides on Earth for the guidance of people, because humanity’s need for guidance is more important than its need for counting the years. [212]

210) Tafsir Rahnama. 211) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 212) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani.

Messages

1- The life of all creatures depends on light and heat, and Allah has provided these two needs through the sun and the moon. «جعل الشمس ضیاءً والقمرنورا»

2- In the movement of celestial bodies and the determination of their orbits, attention has been paid to human needs. «لتعلموا»

3- The order and arrangement in the moon's movement have created a natural, universal, and perpetual calendar. «لتعلموا عدد السنین...» (By observing the changing shape of the moon, one can count the days.)

4- The criterion for calculations in religious matters (Sharī‘ah) is the lunar year and month. «لتعلموا عددالسنین و...»

5- Number, statistics, and calculation play an important role in human life. «لتعلموا عدد السنین و الحساب»

6- Creation is not futile; rather, it is based on truth (Haqq). «بالحقّ»

7- Among the methods of teaching and training is clarifying and elaborating on matters, not speaking vaguely. «یفصّل الآیات»

8- Discovering the secrets of creation requires thought and learning. «لقوم یعلمون»

Ayah

إِنَّ فِى ٱخْتِلَـٰفِ ٱلَّيْلِ وَٱلنَّهَارِ وَمَا خَلَقَ ٱللَّهُ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَتَّقُونَ (٦)
Indeed in the alternation of night and day, and whatever Allah has created in the heavens and the earth, there are surely signs for a people who are Godwary. (6)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the divine signs and the detailing of the verses were stated for the knowledgeable, "لقوم یعلمون" (for a people who know), and in this Ayah, they are stated for the people of piety (Taqwa), "لقوم یتّقون" (for a people who fear God). The conclusion is that those who succeed are the ones who both know and are pious and God-fearing. Otherwise, ignorant pious people or sinful scholars do not benefit sufficiently from the divine signs.

"اِختلاف" (Ikhtilāf) means both coming and going (alternation) and difference (variation). Night and day differ from each other in several respects: A: Their replacement of one another. (213) B: Night is for tranquility and day is for striving. (214) C: The decrease and increase of their hours in different seasons of the year. D: The change in their hours in different regions of the world.

Question: Why do some groups of scientists deny God? Answer: Knowledge alone is not sufficient; rather, perspectives must originate from those who possess purpose and motivation, and from individuals who seek to grasp the truth and reality. "لقوم یتقون" (for a people who fear God).

(213) فمحونا آیة اللیل وجعلنا آیة النهار» اسراء، ۱۲. (214) وجعلنا اللیل لباساً وجعلنا النهار معاشا» نبأ، ۱۰.

Messages

1- The entire existence is in a state of change and transformation. «ان فى الختلاف اللیل و...»

2- We should not pass by phenomena easily. «ماخلق اللّه....لایات»

3- Sin and contamination have a negative effect on human recognition, diagnosis, and analysis. «لایات لقومٍ یتّقون» (Taqwa is the means to correct insight into existence.)

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ لِقَآءَنَا وَرَضُوا۟ بِٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَٱطْمَأَنُّوا۟ بِهَا وَٱلَّذِينَ هُمْ عَنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا غَـٰفِلُونَ (۷)
Indeed those who do not expect to encounter Us and who are pleased with the life of this world and satisfied with it, and those who are oblivious of Our signs (7)
أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ مَأْوَىٰهُمُ ٱلنَّارُ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْسِبُونَ (۸)
—it is they whose refuge shall be the Fire because of what they used to earn. (8)

Key Points

The Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said: «مَن اَحبّ لقاء اللَّه احبّ اللَّهُ لقائه», "Whoever loves meeting Allah, Allah loves meeting him." (215) And in the last Ayah of Surah Al-Kahf we read: «فمن كان یرجو لقاء ربّه فلیعمل عملاً صالحاً» Believers in the Resurrection and the meeting with their Lord must perform righteous deeds.

215) Tafsir Furqan.

Messages

1- Hope and despair play an effective role in the reform and corruption of man. «لایرجون لقاءنا»

2- The more heedless and despairing man becomes of the Resurrection, the more worldly he becomes. Therefore, those who believe in the Resurrection should not be worldly. «لایرجون لقاءنا ورضوا بالحیوة الدنیا»

3- True tranquility is only found through the remembrance of Allah, and the world is a false tranquilizer, especially for the heedless. «اطمأنّوا بها... غافلون»

4- The factors leading to becoming inhabitants of the Fire are:

A: Denying the Resurrection and the rewards of the Hereafter. «لایرجون لقاءنا»

B: Being satisfied with the fleeting worldly life. «رضوا بالحیوة الدنیا»

C: Heedlessness regarding the divine signs (Ayat). «هم عن آیاتنا غافلون»

D: Unacceptable conduct. «بما كانوا یكسبون»

5- Hell is the result of man's own earning. «بما كانوا یكسبون»

Ayah

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ يَهْدِيهِمْ رَبُّهُم بِإِيمَـٰنِهِمْ ۖ تَجْرِى مِن تَحْتِهِمُ ٱلْأَنْهَـٰرُ فِى جَنَّـٰتِ ٱلنَّعِيمِ (۹)
Indeed those who have faith and do righteous deeds, their Lord guides them by the means of their faith. Streams will run for them in gardens of bliss. (9)

Key Points

It is stated in a hadith: When man rises from his grave on the Day of Judgment, his righteous deed is embodied in a luminous form near him, guiding him to Paradise. [216] Divine guidance is a continuous process. Every moment, He increases the guidance of the believers, protects them from pitfalls, and opens a way for them out of the dead ends of this world: «یجعل له مَخرجاً» [217], He illuminates the path for them: «نوراً یمشى به» [218], He resolves their affairs: «اصلح بالهم» [219], He suffices them everywhere: «فهو حسبه» [220], and on the Day of Judgment, He guides them to Paradise: «یسعى نورهم بین ایدیهم» [221]. All these forms of guidance stem from the general concept of «یهدیهم» (He guides them), the specific instance of which He has not specified so that it may encompass all these aspects.

216) Tafsir Namuneh. 217) Talaq, 2. 218) An'am, 122. 219) Muhammad, 2. 220) Talaq, 3. 221) Hadid, 12.

Messages

1- Faith is not separate from action. «آمنوا وعملوا»

2- Although the believer is guided, he is always in need of Divine guidance. [222] «یهدیهم ربّهم»

3- We must ourselves create the grounds for Divine guidance. «یهدیهم ربّهم بایمانهم»

4- In the comparison between faith and action, faith is the axis. «یهدیهم ربّهم بایمانهم» and He did not say: «بایمانهم و عملهم»

5- Allah's guidance toward the believers is absolute and comprehensive. «یهدیهم» is absolute.

6- The Lordship (Rububiyyah) of Allah is the cause of His constant support for the believers. «یهدیهم ربّهم»

7- The rivers of Paradise flow not only beneath the trees but also beneath the houses of Paradise. «من تحتهم الأنهار»

222) Even the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله and the infallible Imams علیهم السلام always used to say: »اهدنا الصراط المستقیم« (Guide us to the straight path).

Ayah

دَعْوَىٰهُمْ فِيهَا سُبْحَـٰنَكَ ٱللَّهُمَّ وَتَحِيَّتُهُمْ فِيهَا سَلَـٰمٌ ۚ وَءَاخِرُ دَعْوَىٰهُمْ أَنِ ٱلْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۱٠)
Their call therein will be, ‘O Allah! Immaculate are You!’ and their greeting therein will be, ‘Peace!’ and their concluding call, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of all the worlds.’ (10)

Key Points

«سلام» (Peace) is the speech of the people of Paradise, and in Paradise, peace fills every space: peace from Allah, «سلام قولاً من ربٍّ رحیم» (223), peace from the angels «سلام علیكم طبتم» (224), and peace among the people of Paradise towards one another. (225) «الاّ قیلاً سلاماً سلاماً» (226)

«الحمد للّه» (Praise be to Allah) is the speech of the prophets and the saints. Prophet Noah عليه السلام said «الحمد للّه» after being saved from the oppressive people (227), and Abraham عليه السلام also praised God for granting him Ishmael and Isaac in his old age. (228) «الحمد للّه» is the speech of the believers (229) and the speech of the people of Paradise.

It is narrated in a Hadith: The people of Paradise call the service providers using the phrase «سبحانك اللّهم», and they immediately appear and fulfill the requests of the people of Paradise. (230)

It is narrated in a Hadith: Whenever a servant is busy praising God and neglects asking Him, Allah grants him more than He would have given him through supplication. (231)

223) Yasin, 58. 224) Zumar, 73. 225) Tafsir Maraghi. 226) Waqi'ah, 26. 227) Mu'minun, 28. 228) Ibrahim, 39. 229) A'raf, 43. 230) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 231) Tafsir Ruh al-Ma'ani.

Messages

  1. Remembrance of God is not only in this world; the Hereafter is also a place for divine remembrance. «سبحانك اللّهم»

  2. Man reaches a point where even his supplication and request become the glorification of the Lord. «دعواهم فیها سبحانك» and this glorification and praise make them successful.

  3. Believers say «سبحانك» regarding the divine attributes, they say «سلام» regarding other believers, and they say «الحمدللَّه» when enjoying the divine blessings.

  4. The believer is a lover of God. Paradise and its pleasures do not distract him from remembering the Beloved; rather, he sees the grace and power of God in everything. «سبحانك اللّهم، الحمد للّه»

  5. The speech of believers begins with «سبحان اللّه» and ends with «الحمدللَّه». (It is inferred from the phrase «آخر دعواهم ان الحمدللَّه» that their first word is «سبحانك الّلهم»)

Ayah

۞ وَلَوْ يُعَجِّلُ ٱللَّهُ لِلنَّاسِ ٱلشَّرَّ ٱسْتِعْجَالَهُم بِٱلْخَيْرِ لَقُضِىَ إِلَيْهِمْ أَجَلُهُمْ ۖ فَنَذَرُ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ لِقَآءَنَا فِى طُغْيَـٰنِهِمْ يَعْمَهُونَ (۱۱)
Were Allah to hasten ill for mankind with their haste for good, their term would have been over. But We leave those who do not expect to encounter Us bewildered in their rebellion. (11)

Key Points

A similar concept to this Ayah is found in Surah Al-Kahf, Ayah 58, and Surah Fatir, Ayah 45, stating that if Allah were to swiftly punish people for their deeds and hold them accountable, everyone would be destroyed. Furthermore, free will, which is the basis of responsibility (taklif), would be eliminated, and obedience would become compulsory. It is possible that the meaning of "استعجالهم بالخیر" (their seeking to hasten the good) is that Allah's tradition (Sunnah) in delivering good is swiftness, while in delivering evil, it involves delay. The meaning of the sentence would then be that Allah is swift and hasty in delivering good.

Messages

1- Man is naturally hasty. "استعجالهم بالخیر" (232)

2- If Allah were to give immediate punishment to the evildoers in this world, no one would remain, and the human race would become extinct. "لو یعجّل اللّه... لقضى الیهم اجلهم"

3- One of the Divine traditions (Sunan) is "Istidraj" (gradual leading to destruction) in punishment and granting respite to the oppressors. "فنذر الذین..."

4- The non-destruction of the disbelievers is not a sign of their righteousness. "فنذر الذین لایرجون لقاءنا"

5- Those who have cut themselves off from Allah and become preoccupied with themselves have no definite goal and are bewildered. "فى طغیانهم یعمهون"

6- Denying and ignoring the Day of Judgment leads to transgression (tughyan), being left to their own devices, and being deprived of Divine favors. "لایرجون لقاءنا فى طغیانهم یعمهون"

(232) It is stated in another Ayah: "خُلِق الانسان من عجل" (Man was created of haste). Al-Anbiya, 37.

Ayah

وَإِذَا مَسَّ ٱلْإِنسَـٰنَ ٱلضُّرُّ دَعَانَا لِجَنۢبِهِۦٓ أَوْ قَاعِدًا أَوْ قَآئِمًا فَلَمَّا كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُ ضُرَّهُۥ مَرَّ كَأَن لَّمْ يَدْعُنَآ إِلَىٰ ضُرٍّ مَّسَّهُۥ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ زُيِّنَ لِلْمُسْرِفِينَ مَا كَانُوا۟ يَعْمَلُونَ (۱۲)
When distress befalls man, he supplicates Us, [lying] on his side, sitting, or standing; but when We remove his distress, he passes on as if he had never supplicated Us concerning the distress that had befallen him. What they have been doing is thus presented as decorous to the transgressors. (12)

Key Points

Many times Allah has complained about human ingratitude, where man calls upon Him during distress and hardship, but after the problem is solved, he becomes heedless of Allah and forgets Him.

Messages

1- Belief in Allah is intertwined in the depth of the human soul, and bitter events are the factor that awakens this conscience and his God-seeking nature (fitrah). «مسّ الانسان الضّر دعانا»

2- Those whose prayers and supplications are seasonal are subject to criticism and their deeds are worthless. «مس الانسان الضّر دعانا»

3- Supplication (Du'a) is permissible in every state: standing, sitting, or lying down. «جنبه، قاعداً، قائماً»

4- Sincere supplication is the cause of the removal of distress. «دعانا... كشفنا»

5- Prosperity is the ground for heedlessness. «فلمّا كشفنا عنه مرّ كان لم یدعنا»

6- Man is ungrateful. «مرّ كان لم یدعنا»

7- For those who forget Allah, material life appears beautiful in their eyes. «زیّن للمسرفین»

8- Forgetting the favors of Allah is a type of extravagance (isrāf). «مرّ... للمسرفین»

9- Anyone who is heedless in prosperity and prays only in distress is extravagant (musrif). «مسّ الانسان الضّر دعانا... فلمّا كشفنا عنه مرّ... زیّن للمسرفین»

10- The affluent are self-satisfied. «زیّن للمسرفین ما كانوا یعملون»

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ أَهْلَكْنَا ٱلْقُرُونَ مِن قَبْلِكُمْ لَمَّا ظَلَمُوا۟ ۙ وَجَآءَتْهُمْ رُسُلُهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ لِيُؤْمِنُوا۟ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَجْزِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلْمُجْرِمِينَ (۱۳)
Certainly We destroyed generations before you when they perpetrated wrongs: their apostles brought them manifest proofs, but they would not have faith. Thus do We requite the guilty lot. (13)

Key Points

Qurūn (قُرون) is the plural of Qarn (قَرن), and it refers to people who live in the same era and time.

Messages

1- Oppression and tyranny pave the way for the downfall of nations. «اهلكنا القرون... لمّا ظلموا»

2- Divine punishment and wrath occur after the completion of the argument (proof). «اهلكنا وجائتهم رسلهم بالبیّنات»

3- Destruction is for the oppressors whose faith and reform are hopeless. «وما كانوا لیؤمنوا»

4- Alongside history, the philosophy of history must also be stated so that it serves as a lesson. [233] «اهلكنا القرون ... كذلك نجزى»

5- Divine traditions (Sunan) are universal and perpetual. «كذلك نجزى القوم المجرمین»

233) The destruction of past nations is history, and the secret of that destruction, which is oppression and crime, is the philosophy of history.

Ayah

ثُمَّ جَعَلْنَـٰكُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ مِنۢ بَعْدِهِمْ لِنَنظُرَ كَيْفَ تَعْمَلُونَ (۱٤)
Then We made you successors on the earth after them that We may observe how you will act. (14)

Messages

1- Governance and enablement are means of Divine testing. «جعلناكم خلائف ... لننظر»

2- Every manager who delegates responsibility must also supervise. «جعلناكم، لننظر»

3- Our performance is under the supervision of Allah. «لننظر»

4- All nations are equal before Allah, and the law of Divine testing applies to everyone. «لننظر كیف تعملون»

5- The destiny of people is the result of their own actions. «كیف تعملون»

Ayah

وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَاتُنَا بَيِّنَـٰتٍ ۙ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَرْجُونَ لِقَآءَنَا ٱئْتِ بِقُرْءَانٍ غَيْرِ هَـٰذَآ أَوْ بَدِّلْهُ ۚ قُلْ مَا يَكُونُ لِىٓ أَنْ أُبَدِّلَهُۥ مِن تِلْقَآئِ نَفْسِىٓ ۖ إِنْ أَتَّبِعُ إِلَّا مَا يُوحَىٰٓ إِلَىَّ ۖ إِنِّىٓ أَخَافُ إِنْ عَصَيْتُ رَبِّى عَذَابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (۱٥)
When Our manifest signs are recited to them, those who do not expect to encounter Us say, ‘Bring a Quran other than this, or alter it.’ Say, ‘I may not alter it of my own accord. I follow only what is revealed to me. Indeed should I disobey my Lord, I fear the punishment of a tremendous day.’ (15)

Key Points

Some idolaters requested the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله to change some verses of this Quran which criticize their idols, or to bring another Quran! Those who found the heavenly teachings contrary to their desires have always made such requests.

Messages

1- The verses and signs of divine recognition must be explained to the people. «اذا تُتلى...»

2- No one, not even the Prophet, can change the divine law. «مایكون لى ان ابدّله»

3- We must never surrender to the excuses and desires of the people. The principle is what Allah says, not what the people want. «ما یكون لى ان ابدّله»

4- No human being, not even the Prophets, had a hand in the Quran. «من تلقاء نفسى»

5- Increasing the number of followers is worthless if it comes at the cost of changing, distorting the doctrine, or abandoning principles. (Considering the occasion of revelation)

6- Prophets are infallible (ma'sum) and follow nothing but revelation. «ان اتّبع الا ما یوحى»

7- Remembering the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is a factor for piety (taqwa) and immunity from sin. «انّى اخاف ان عصیت ربّى عذاب یوم عظیم», just as heedlessness of the Resurrection is a factor for making excuses. «لایرجون لقاءنا»

8- The source of the infallibility of the Prophets is faith and certainty in the Resurrection. «انى اخاف ان عصیت ربّى عذاب یوم عظیم»

Ayah

قُل لَّوْ شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ مَا تَلَوْتُهُۥ عَلَيْكُمْ وَلَآ أَدْرَىٰكُم بِهِۦ ۖ فَقَدْ لَبِثْتُ فِيكُمْ عُمُرًا مِّن قَبْلِهِۦٓ ۚ أَفَلَا تَعْقِلُونَ (۱٦)
Say, ‘Had Allah [so] wished, I would not have recited it to you, nor would He have made it known to you, for I have dwelled among you for a lifetime before it. Do you not exercise your reason?’ (16)

Key Points

The address of the verse is directed at those requesting changes to the Quran, and it states: The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله lived among you for forty years; if the Quran were the product of his own thoughts, other examples of his ideas should have been uttered by him during this period.

Messages

1- The Prophet of God does nothing without the permission and will of Allah. «لوشاء اللَّه ماتلوته»

2- The Quran is a divine miracle, not the product of human thought. «ماتلوته... ولاادریكم به»

3- The Prophet’s history of purity, trustworthiness, and unlettered status is a sign of His truthfulness. «فقد لبثت فیكم عمراً ... افلا تعقلون»

Ayah

فَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ ٱفْتَرَىٰ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ كَذِبًا أَوْ كَذَّبَ بِـَٔايَـٰتِهِۦٓ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ (۱۷)
So who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah, or denies His signs? Indeed the guilty will not be felicitous. (17)

Key Points

The Ayah is a continuation of the response to those who requested the Prophet to change and alter the Quran, and this request is considered a form of fabrication (slander) against Allah. A similar Ayah exists in other instances, including: «وَمن اظلم ممّن افترى عَلى اللَّه كَذِباً و قالَ اُوحِىَ الىّ ولم یُوحَ الیه شیئٌ» [234], which considers the false claim of revelation as lying against Allah.

234) Al-An'am (The Cattle), 93.

Messages

1- The greatest injustice is cultural and ideological injustice and the distortion of the verses (Ayat). «من اظلم»

2- The greater the personality, the more dangerous the slander against Him. «اظلم ممّن افترى على اللَّه»

3- Those who invent religion (fabricators of faith) are on the same level as the disbeliever. «افترى، كذّب»

4- Innovation (in religion/Bid'ah) is a crime. «المجرمون»

5- If a religious preacher presents his own thoughts in the name of religion, he deserves epithets such as «اظلم», «لایفلح» and «مجرم».

Ayah

وَيَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَضُرُّهُمْ وَلَا يَنفَعُهُمْ وَيَقُولُونَ هَـٰٓؤُلَآءِ شُفَعَـٰٓؤُنَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ ۚ قُلْ أَتُنَبِّـُٔونَ ٱللَّهَ بِمَا لَا يَعْلَمُ فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَلَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ وَتَعَـٰلَىٰ عَمَّا يُشْرِكُونَ (۱۸)
They worship besides Allah that which neither causes them any harm, nor brings them any benefit, and they say, ‘These are our intercessors with Allah.’ Say, ‘Will you inform Allah about something He does not know in the heavens or on the earth?’ Immaculate is He and exalted above [having] any partners that they ascribe [to Him]! (18)

Key Points

Tafsir al-Maraghi, by relying on this Ayah, has rejected Tawassul (seeking intercession) through the Awliya (friends of God) and visiting graves. While, firstly: the word "yā'budūn" (they worship) is used in this Ayah, and in visiting (graves) and Tawassul, we do not worship anyone. Secondly: we believe in the life of Barzakh (the intermediate realm), and the Quran also considers the martyrs to be alive. We ask the Awliya of Allah who have been martyred in the way of Allah for prayer (supplication). Comparing them and the martyrs with stone and wood is unfair. The worship of idols is either due to fear or the hope of benefit. Idols neither cause harm that they should be worshipped out of fear, nor do they bring benefit that they should be worthy of worship; thus, there is no excuse for idol worship. «لاینفعهم ولایضرّهم»

Messages

1- Idolaters lack logic, and idol worship is an illogical act. «یعبدون... لایضرهم ولاینفعهم»

2- Idolaters accepted Allah, but they considered the idols to be intermediaries, and therefore they worshipped the idols. «هؤلاء شفعاؤنا»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ ٱلنَّاسُ إِلَّآ أُمَّةً وَٰحِدَةً فَٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ ۚ وَلَوْلَا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِن رَّبِّكَ لَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُمْ فِيمَا فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ (۱۹)
Mankind were but a single [religious] community; then they differed. And were it not for a prior decree of your Lord, decision would have been made between them concerning that about which they differ. (19)

Key Points

Allah, when Adam and Eve descended to the earth, said: «و لكم فى الارض مستقرّ و متاع الى حین» (235) (The earth is a dwelling place for you and your progeny for a period.) Therefore, from the very first day, it was decreed that man would have a respite on earth for a period. Thus, perhaps the intent of the phrase «كلمة سبقت» (the word that preceded) in this Ayah is that very decree and program for human life.

235) Baqarah, 36.

Messages

1- Humanity, from the beginning, was a single nation (Ummah), united in thought and belief regarding Monotheism (Tawhid). «ما كانَ النّاس الاّ امّة واحِدة»

2- Man has had a social life from the very beginning. «امّة»

3- Difference in belief and action is a requirement of human societies. «امّة واحدة فاختلفوا»

4- Granting respite is among the affairs of Divine Lordship (Rububiyyah). «سبقت من ربّك»

5- The Divine tradition (Sunnah) is to grant individuals respite for choice and action. «لولا كلمة... لقضى»

Ayah

وَيَقُولُونَ لَوْلَآ أُنزِلَ عَلَيْهِ ءَايَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّهِۦ ۖ فَقُلْ إِنَّمَا ٱلْغَيْبُ لِلَّهِ فَٱنتَظِرُوٓا۟ إِنِّى مَعَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُنتَظِرِينَ (۲٠)
They say, ‘Why has not some sign been sent down to him from his Lord?’ Say, ‘[The knowledge of] the Unseen belongs only to Allah. So wait. I too am waiting along with you.’ (20)

Messages

1- Those who make excuses ignore the Qur'an and hundreds of its verses, demanding another sign. «لولا أنزل علیه آیة»

2- Miracles occur by the Will of Allah; they are neither at the disposal of the Prophet nor subject to the whims and desires of the people. «انّما الغیب للَّه»

3- The tradition and law of Allah is to grant respite. «فانتظروا ...»

Ayah

وَإِذَآ أَذَقْنَا ٱلنَّاسَ رَحْمَةً مِّنۢ بَعْدِ ضَرَّآءَ مَسَّتْهُمْ إِذَا لَهُم مَّكْرٌ فِىٓ ءَايَاتِنَا ۚ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ أَسْرَعُ مَكْرًا ۚ إِنَّ رُسُلَنَا يَكْتُبُونَ مَا تَمْكُرُونَ (۲۱)
When We let people taste [Our] mercy after a distress that has befallen them, behold, they scheme against Our signs! Say, ‘Allah is more swift at devising.’ Indeed Our messengers write down what you scheme. (21)

Key Points

The previous Ayah referred to their excuses, where they said: Why is no Ayah being revealed? Here, it addresses their obstinacy, that even after the Ayah is revealed, instead of appreciating it, they scheme. A drought and famine occurred in Mecca, and Allah sent rain through the blessing of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله. But the polytheists said that the rain was due to the idols! This Ayah was revealed and exposed them. [236] It is mentioned in most commentaries (tafasir) that one of the schemes and plots of the enemies was to undermine the personality of the Prophet and ridicule the Divine verses (Ayat).

236) Tafasir Safi, Fi Zilal al-Quran, and Kabir Fakhr Razi.

Messages

1- The blessings that man utilizes are but a part of the boundless Divine Mercy. «اذقنا الناس رحمة»

2- Man misuses Divine blessings and, instead of showing gratitude, he plots. «اذقنا الناس رحمة....اذا لهم مكرٌ»

3- Whoever plots will be afflicted by the forceful Divine scheme and plot. «اذا لهم مكر... اللَّه اسرع مكرا»

4- Divine retribution is commensurate with the crime. «لهم مكر، اللّه اسرع مكراً»

5- The Divine angels record even the thoughts and schemes of man. «یكتبون ماتمكرون»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى يُسَيِّرُكُمْ فِى ٱلْبَرِّ وَٱلْبَحْرِ ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَا كُنتُمْ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ وَجَرَيْنَ بِهِم بِرِيحٍ طَيِّبَةٍ وَفَرِحُوا۟ بِهَا جَآءَتْهَا رِيحٌ عَاصِفٌ وَجَآءَهُمُ ٱلْمَوْجُ مِن كُلِّ مَكَانٍ وَظَنُّوٓا۟ أَنَّهُمْ أُحِيطَ بِهِمْ ۙ دَعَوُا۟ ٱللَّهَ مُخْلِصِينَ لَهُ ٱلدِّينَ لَئِنْ أَنجَيْتَنَا مِنْ هَـٰذِهِۦ لَنَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلشَّـٰكِرِينَ (۲۲)
It is He who carries you across land and sea. When you are in the ships, and they sail along with them with a favourable wind, exulting in it, there comes upon them a tempestuous wind and waves assail them from every side, and they think that they are besieged, they invoke Allah putting exclusive faith in Him, ‘If You deliver us from this, we will surely be among the grateful.’ (22)

Key Points

Alusi and Maraghi, in their commentaries, have stated that Shias call upon their Imams during times of danger, whereas polytheists call upon Allah during times of danger!! They are oblivious to the fact that seeking intercession (Tawassul) through an infallible martyr Imam, who, according to the Quran, is alive, is different from seeking intercession through an idol.

Messages

1- The laws governing nature are created and subject to Allah. «هو الذى یسیّركم»

2- Human actions are also attributed to Allah, because power originates from Him. Although movement (sīr) is a human action, «سیروا فى الارض», yet He says: «هو الّذى یسیّركم»

3- No matter how much progress man makes, he is not safe from the harm of natural disasters. «جاءتها ریح عاصف»

4- The affluent should not imagine that they will always remain in comfort. «فرحوابها، احیط بهم»

5- Natural disasters remove human arrogance and pride, making him humble before Allah. «دَعَوا اللَّه»

6- In times of danger, man's innate nature turns toward a single source of salvation. «دعوا اللَّه مخلصین»

7- Faith and sincerity must be permanent, not seasonal or only when danger is felt. «احیط بهم دعوا اللّه مخلصین»

8- Man makes promises during times of danger, but becomes heedless when he reaches comfort. «لئن انجیتنا»

9- Ingratitude and denial of blessings are among the causes for the emergence of hardship and punishment. «لئن انجیتنا من هذه لنكوننّ من الشاكرین»

Ayah

فَلَمَّآ أَنجَىٰهُمْ إِذَا هُمْ يَبْغُونَ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ بِغَيْرِ ٱلْحَقِّ ۗ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِنَّمَا بَغْيُكُمْ عَلَىٰٓ أَنفُسِكُم ۖ مَّتَـٰعَ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا ۖ ثُمَّ إِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُكُمْ فَنُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۲۳)
But when He delivers them, behold, they commit violations on the earth unduly! O mankind! Your violations are only to your own detriment. [These are] the wares of the life of this world; then to Us will be your return, whereat We will inform you concerning what you used to do. (23)

Key Points

«بَغى» means seeking. Because the oppressor (ẓālim) always desires the rights of others, he is called a bāghī (transgressor). [237]

237) Tafsir Al-Mizan.

Messages

1- Man is disloyal, a promise-breaker, and ungrateful. «فلمَّا أنجاهُم ، یَبغون» (In the previous Ayah, he promised to be thankful if He saved him, but after salvation, he shows ingratitude.)

2- Oppression against people is oppression against oneself, because we are all from one another. [238] «بغیكم على انفسكم»

3- The punishment for oppression is both in this world and the Hereafter. «على انفسكم، الینا مرجعكم»

4- Oppressors only enjoy success for a few days in this world, which is insignificant compared to the punishment of the Day of Judgment. «متاع الحیاة الدنیا»

5- The root of oppression is man's pursuit of the world. «متاع الحیاة الدنیا»

6- The remembrance of the Day of Judgment is one of the factors that prevents sin. «یاایّها الناس... الینامرجعكم»

7- Allah is aware of all of man's deeds, and man will also become aware of all his deeds on the Day of Judgment. «فننبّئكم بما كنتم تعملون»

238) بعضكم من بعض» Āl ‘Imrān, 195.

Ayah

إِنَّمَا مَثَلُ ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا كَمَآءٍ أَنزَلْنَـٰهُ مِنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ فَٱخْتَلَطَ بِهِۦ نَبَاتُ ٱلْأَرْضِ مِمَّا يَأْكُلُ ٱلنَّاسُ وَٱلْأَنْعَـٰمُ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَخَذَتِ ٱلْأَرْضُ زُخْرُفَهَا وَٱزَّيَّنَتْ وَظَنَّ أَهْلُهَآ أَنَّهُمْ قَـٰدِرُونَ عَلَيْهَآ أَتَىٰهَآ أَمْرُنَا لَيْلًا أَوْ نَهَارًا فَجَعَلْنَـٰهَا حَصِيدًا كَأَن لَّمْ تَغْنَ بِٱلْأَمْسِ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نُفَصِّلُ ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتِ لِقَوْمٍ يَتَفَكَّرُونَ (۲٤)
The parable of the life of this world is that of water which We send down from the sky. It mingles with the earth’s vegetation from which humans and cattle eat. When the earth puts on its luster and is adorned, and its inhabitants think they have power over it, Our edict comes to it, by night or day, whereat We turn it into a mown field, as if it did not flourish the day before. Thus do We elaborate the signs for a people who reflect. (24)

Key Points

The previous Ayah considered the world a trivial possession, and this Ayah illustrates it with a beautiful parable. In some narrations, this Ayah is considered a symbol of the advent (Zuhur) of Imam Mahdi عليه السلام, indicating that whenever the earth becomes submerged in glitter, glamour, and materialism, everything will change by Divine command and a doctrinal revolution. [239]

239) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Parables are among the best methods of propagation (Da'wah) and education. «انّما مثل الحَیاة الدّنیا»

2- The best parables are those taken from nature, which time and place cannot render obsolete. «انّما مثل الحَیاة الدّنیا كماء»

3- In utilizing the material bounties of nature, humans and animals are equal, but the value of humanity lies in utilizing spiritual matters. «یأكل الناس والأنعام»

4- The suitability of vegetation for the needs of humans and animals is among the Divine favors. «نبات الارض یأكل الناس و الانعام»

5- The adornment of humanity is faith [240], and the adornment of the earth is its plants. «ازّیّنت»

6- The world deceives humanity and leads them to mere imagination and assumption. «ظنّ اهلها انهم قادرون»

7- Just as the growth of plants is in the hand of Allah [241], so too is their drying up and destruction in His hand. «جعلناها حصیداً»

8- The world and its manifestations are exposed to all kinds of calamities. «ازّیّنت... حصیداً»

9- Human life in the world, like the life of greenery and flowers, is short and fleeting. «مثل الحَیاة الدّنیا... كأن لم تغن بالامس»

10- The wrath of Allah is sometimes so severe that it leaves no trace behind. «لم تغن بالأمس»

11- The parables of the Quran are a basis for contemplation, so we should not take them lightly. «لقومٍ یتفكّرون»

12- Attachment to and being deceived by the world is a sign of short-sightedness, while not being deceived is a sign of wisdom and thought. «لقومٍ یتفكّرون»

240) «حبّب الیكم الایمان و زیّنه فى قلوبكم» (Al-Hujurat, 7).

241) «ءَانتم تزرعونه ام نحن الزارعون» (Al-Waqi'ah, 64).

Ayah

وَٱللَّهُ يَدْعُوٓا۟ إِلَىٰ دَارِ ٱلسَّلَـٰمِ وَيَهْدِى مَن يَشَآءُ إِلَىٰ صِرَٰطٍ مُّسْتَقِيمٍ (۲٥)
Allah invites to the abode of peace, and He guides whomever He wishes to a straight path. (25)

Key Points

«دارالسلام» (Dār al-Salām) is one of the names of Paradise, because there is no malice in the hearts there, which would lead to war, dispute, and exploitation. «ونزعنا ما فى صدورهم من غلّ» (242) Salām is a Name of Allah, and Dār al-Salām means the proximity of Divine Mercy and a place where their speech and greeting is Salām, and Salām is also bestowed upon them by Allah. «سلام قولاً من ربّ رحیم» (243) In narrations, the path of the Wilāyah (guardianship) of Amīr al-Mu'minīn عليه السلام and the infallible heavenly leaders has been stated as the best example of the straight path.

(242) Al-A'rāf, 43. (243) Yā Sīn, 58.

Messages

1- The Divine invitation is a sign of His special favor upon His servants. «واللّه یدعوا»

2- Worldly safety is transient, but the safety of the Hereafter is permanent. «دار السّلام»

3- The Straight Path (Sirāt al-Mustaqīm) is a source of tranquility, and besides the Hereafter, it also makes this world Dār al-Salām (Abode of Peace) for man. «یدعوا الى دار السّلام»

4- A healthy life and society is the objective of Allah. «یدعوا الى دارالسّلام»

5- Allah both invites and shows the way. «یدعوا، یهدى»

6- The Divine invitation is for everyone, but special guidance is exclusive to those whom He wills. «یهدى من یشاء»

7- Movement upon the Straight Path requires Divine success (Tawfīq). «یهدى...الى صراط مستقیم»

Ayah

۞ لِّلَّذِينَ أَحْسَنُوا۟ ٱلْحُسْنَىٰ وَزِيَادَةٌ ۖ وَلَا يَرْهَقُ وُجُوهَهُمْ قَتَرٌ وَلَا ذِلَّةٌ ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلْجَنَّةِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۲٦)
Those who are virtuous shall receive the best reward and an enhancement. Neither dust nor abasement shall overcast their faces. They shall be the inhabitants of paradise, and they shall remain in it [forever]. (26)

Key Points

«Rahaq» means compulsory covering, and «qatar» means dust, smoke, and ash. The granting of greater and multiplied reward is repeatedly mentioned in the Quran using the terms «Ziyādah» (increase), «Ḍi‘f» (double), and «Aḍ‘āf» (multiples). Examples include: whoever performs a good deed shall have ten times its like, [244] Allah grants them from His bounty in addition to the full reward, [245] and a reward of seven hundred times for spending in the way of Allah. [246] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام says: Everything has a measure, except tears, a drop of which extinguishes fires. Then He said: Whoever sheds a tear for Allah, his face will not see darkness or humiliation. «لا یرهق وجوههم قترٌ ولاذلّة» [247] In some narrations, the meaning of «Ziyādah» has been taken to mean the world (this life). [248] And many narrations from the Ahl al-Sunnah consider «Ziyādah» to be meeting with Allah (and His favors). [249] «Aḥsanū» (they who do good), the scope of goodness includes good belief, worthy action, and good speech.

244) An'am, 160. 245) Nisa, 173. 246) Baqarah, 261. 247) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 248) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 249) Tafsir Durr al-Manthur.

Messages

1- Receiving Divine favors is exclusive to the doers of good. «للذین احسنوا الحسنى»

2- Allah gives both the reward for the deed and encouragement (an increase). «الحسنى وزیادة»

3- The reward for the doers of good on the Day of Judgment is the best of rewards. «للذین احسنوا الحسنى»

4- Those who turn to other than Allah have no excuse, because Allah both invites, «یدعوا الى دارالسلام», and guides, «یهدى من یشاء», and gives recompense, «الحسنى», and gives an increase, «زیادة», and His increase is very significant. [250]

5- Goodness and benevolence in the fleeting and transient world lead to life in eternal Paradise. «احسنوا... هم فیها خالدون»

250) The word «Ziyādah» is indefinite (nakerah), which is a sign of its greatness.

Ayah

وَٱلَّذِينَ كَسَبُوا۟ ٱلسَّيِّـَٔاتِ جَزَآءُ سَيِّئَةٍۭ بِمِثْلِهَا وَتَرْهَقُهُمْ ذِلَّةٌ ۖ مَّا لَهُم مِّنَ ٱللَّهِ مِنْ عَاصِمٍ ۖ كَأَنَّمَآ أُغْشِيَتْ وُجُوهُهُمْ قِطَعًا مِّنَ ٱلَّيْلِ مُظْلِمًا ۚ أُو۟لَـٰٓئِكَ أَصْحَـٰبُ ٱلنَّارِ ۖ هُمْ فِيهَا خَـٰلِدُونَ (۲۷)
For those who have committed misdeeds, the requital of a misdeed shall be its like, and they shall be overcast by abasement. They shall have no one to protect [them] from Allah. [They will be] as if their faces were covered with dark patches of the night. They shall be the inmates of the Fire, and they shall remain in it [forever]. (27)

Key Points

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: This Ayah concerns the people of innovation (bid'ah), doubts (shubuhat), and desires (shahawat). [251] Layl-i Muẓlim (dark night) is a night where there is no sign of moonlight, which occurs one night every month. This is in contrast to Layl-i Muqmir (moonlit night), whose moonlight is bright for everyone. By the evidence of Ayahs such as: «وجوه يومئذٍ عليها غبرة. ترهقها قترة. أولئك هم الكفرة الفجرة» [252] and the Ayah «فأمّا الّذينَ اسودّت وجوههم أكفرتم بعد إيمانكم» [253], it can be said that the phrase «أُغشيت وجوههم قطعاً من الليل» (their faces will be covered by a portion of the night) in this Ayah refers to the disbelievers (kuffār).

251) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 252) Abasa, 40 - 42. 253) Aal-i Imran, 106.

Messages

1- Humans approach sin by choice, not compulsion. «كسبوا»

2- Allah’s treatment of evildoers is just. «جزاء سيّئة بمثلها»

3- This world is the abode of action, and the Hereafter is the abode of reward. «والذين كسبوا... جزاء»

4- Encouragement must outweigh punishment and retribution. Regarding the righteous, He said: «الحسنى و زيادة» (the best reward and increase), and regarding the evildoers, He said: «سيئةٌ بمثلها» (an evil deed is its equal).

5- Blackening of the face (disgrace) is specific to the disbelievers (kuffār), not sinful believers. «ترهقهم ذلّة»

6- On the Day of Judgment, there is both punishment and retribution, as well as humiliation and disgrace. «سيئة بمثلها، أغشيت وجوههم...»

7- The path of escape, salvation, and intercession is closed to the disbelievers. «مالهم من اللّه من عاصم»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ نَحْشُرُهُمْ جَمِيعًا ثُمَّ نَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ أَشْرَكُوا۟ مَكَانَكُمْ أَنتُمْ وَشُرَكَآؤُكُمْ ۚ فَزَيَّلْنَا بَيْنَهُمْ ۖ وَقَالَ شُرَكَآؤُهُم مَّا كُنتُمْ إِيَّانَا تَعْبُدُونَ (۲۸)
On the day when We gather them all together, We shall say to those who ascribe partners [to Allah], ‘Stay where you are—you and your partners!’ Then We shall set them apart from one another, and their partners will say, ‘It was not us that you worshipped. (28)

Key Points

The previous Ayah discussed that there is no path to salvation for the criminals and disbelievers, and this Ayah states that even the imaginary intercessors will disavow them instead of interceding for them.

Messages

1- Let us not forget the Day of Resurrection and its events. «یوم نحشرهم جمیعا»

2- The Day of Resurrection is not exclusive to humans; rather, all fabricated deities will also be present. «انتم وشركاءكم»

3- The Day of Resurrection is the day of separation. «زیّلنا بینهم» (On the Day of Resurrection, separation occurs among people for the purpose of interrogation.)

4- Those whom the disbelievers imagined to be their intercessors will disavow them on the Day of Resurrection. «ما كنتم ایّانا تعبدون»

5- On the Day of Resurrection, idols will possess consciousness and speech, and they will deny their worshippers or express ignorance of their worship. «ما كنتم ایّانا تعبدون»

Ayah

فَكَفَىٰ بِٱللَّهِ شَهِيدًۢا بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكُمْ إِن كُنَّا عَنْ عِبَادَتِكُمْ لَغَـٰفِلِينَ (۲۹)
Allah suffices as a witness between you and us. We were indeed unaware of your worship.’ (29)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the worshipped entities (ma'būds) said that you were not worshipping us. Here, they say: We were unaware of your worship. The conclusion is that any act of worship of which the worshipped entity is unaware is not worship. The issue of the worshipped entities expressing aversion and disassociation (bara'at) from the polytheists and their actions has been mentioned repeatedly in the Quran. Including: Ayah 17 of Surah Al-Furqan and Ayah 63 of Surah Al-Qasas.

Messages

1- Idols will gain consciousness and speech on the Day of Judgment. «فكفى باللّه شهیدا»

Ayah

هُنَالِكَ تَبْلُوا۟ كُلُّ نَفْسٍ مَّآ أَسْلَفَتْ ۚ وَرُدُّوٓا۟ إِلَى ٱللَّهِ مَوْلَىٰهُمُ ٱلْحَقِّ ۖ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُم مَّا كَانُوا۟ يَفْتَرُونَ (۳٠)
There every soul will examine what it has sent in advance, and they will be returned to Allah, their real master, and what they used to fabricate will forsake them. (30)

Messages

1- The Day of Resurrection is the day of receiving the report card of the worldly test. «تبلوا كل نفس»

2- The reckoning on the Day of Resurrection applies to all human beings. «تبلوا كل نفسٍ»

3- The source of existence is He, and the end of the affair is towards Him. «ردّوا» (254)

4- The true Master (Mawla) is Allah, and whatever is other than Him is futile. «مولاهم الحقّ»

5- On the Day of Resurrection, the false manifestations of the worshipped objects (deities) will vanish. «ضلّ عنهم»

(254) «ردّوا» is used in cases where the beginning also originated from that source.

Ayah

قُلْ مَن يَرْزُقُكُم مِّنَ ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ أَمَّن يَمْلِكُ ٱلسَّمْعَ وَٱلْأَبْصَـٰرَ وَمَن يُخْرِجُ ٱلْحَىَّ مِنَ ٱلْمَيِّتِ وَيُخْرِجُ ٱلْمَيِّتَ مِنَ ٱلْحَىِّ وَمَن يُدَبِّرُ ٱلْأَمْرَ ۚ فَسَيَقُولُونَ ٱللَّهُ ۚ فَقُلْ أَفَلَا تَتَّقُونَ (۳۱)
Say, ‘Who provides for you out of the sky and the earth? Who controls [your] hearing and sight, and who brings forth the living from the dead and brings forth the dead from the living, and who directs the command?’ They will say, ‘Allah.’ Say, ‘Will you not then be wary [of Him]?’ (31)

Key Points

Regarding the act of bringing forth the dead from the living and the living from the dead, which is the work of Allah, examples have been cited, including: the emergence of man from sperm and sperm from man, the chick from the egg and the egg from the chick, the plant from the seed and the seed from the plant, the righteous child from corrupt parents, and the corrupt child from worthy parents. The fact that the Ayah mentions only the eye and the ear among all human organs is perhaps because these two organs represent the most effective faculties among the body's members.

Messages

1- The method of questioning and prompting humans to think and refer back to their innate nature (fitrah) is among the duties of divine educators. «قل من یرزقكم»

2- Allah has involved the heavens and the earth in providing sustenance (rizq) for man. «یرزقكم من السماء و الارض»

3- Providing sustenance, creating, and managing are among the permanent divine practices. «یرزق، یملك، یخرج، یدبّر» (The present tense verb signifies continuity.)

4- The universe constantly requires management (tadbir), and the unity of management is a sign of the unity of Lordship (Rububiyyah). «من یدبّر الامر»

5- Our sustenance, our hearing and seeing, our life and our death—all are in the hands of Allah, and no one or nothing besides Him possesses power or sovereignty in these areas. «مَن یَملكُ السَّمع و الابصار... فسیقولون اللّه»

6- Reflecting upon divine blessings and power is one of the sweetest, most tangible, most universal, simplest, and most love-inducing paths to knowing Allah. «قل من یرزقكم... فسیقولون اللّه»

7- Even the polytheists inherently know Allah to be the Creator of existence. «فسیقولون اللَّه»

8- Mere belief is not enough; action is also necessary. «فسیقولون اللَّه، فقل افلاتتّقون»

9- Recognition and knowledge pave the way for piety (taqwa). «فسیقولون اللَّه، فقل افلاتتّقون»

Ayah

فَذَٰلِكُمُ ٱللَّهُ رَبُّكُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۖ فَمَاذَا بَعْدَ ٱلْحَقِّ إِلَّا ٱلضَّلَـٰلُ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُصْرَفُونَ (۳۲)
That, then, is Allah, your true Lord. So what is there after the truth except error? Then where are you being led away? (32)

Messages

1- Given the existence of the Sovereign, Provider, Creator, and Planner God (as mentioned in the previous Ayah), there is no room for Lordship other than Him. «فذلكم اللَّه ربّكم الحقّ فماذا بعدالحق»

2- Belief in a Lordship other than Allah is falsehood and misguidance. «فماذا بعدالحقّ الاالضّلال»

3- We have no other path between truth (Haqq) and falsehood (Batil); rather, whatever is not truth is falsehood, and neutrality or being unaligned is rejected. «فماذا بعد الحقّ الاّ الضّلال»

4- Man possesses a truth-seeking nature (Fitrah). Satans, delusions, and other factors lead him to deviation. «تُصرفون»

Ayah

كَذَٰلِكَ حَقَّتْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ فَسَقُوٓا۟ أَنَّهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۳۳)
Thus the word of your Lord became due against those who transgress, that they shall not have faith. (33)

Messages

1- Man, by choosing the path of transgression (fisq), makes the Divine wrath certain for himself. «حقّت ... على الّذین فسقوا»

2- Transgression (fisq) and sin are obstacles to inclination towards faith (Iman) and are the groundwork for disbelief (kufr). «فسقوا... لایؤمنون»

Ayah

قُلْ هَلْ مِن شُرَكَآئِكُم مَّن يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ ۚ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ يَبْدَؤُا۟ ٱلْخَلْقَ ثُمَّ يُعِيدُهُۥ ۖ فَأَنَّىٰ تُؤْفَكُونَ (۳٤)
Say, ‘Is there anyone among your partners who originates the creation and then brings it back?’ Say, ‘Allah originates the creation, then He will bring it back.’ Then where do you stray? (34)

Messages

1- Allah teaches His Prophet the method of debate and argumentation with the polytheists. «قل»

2- The partner ascribed to Allah is nothing but the fabrication of human illusions. «شركائكم»

3- Existence is contingent and a phenomenon. «یبدؤا الخلق»

4- The criterion for worship is two things: one is the ability to originate creation, and the other is the power to restore it. «یبدؤا... ثمّ یعیده»

5- Resurrection occurs after the extinction of the world. «یبدؤا... ثمّ یعیده»

6- Existence is moving towards death and extinction, and its ultimate end is death and non-existence. «یبدؤا... ثمّ یعیده»

7- The Resurrection (Ma'ad) and Qiyamah are similar to this world and are physical (corporeal). «ثمّ یعیده»

8- Ascribing partners (to Allah) is a sign of folly. «فانّى تؤفكون»

Ayah

قُلْ هَلْ مِن شُرَكَآئِكُم مَّن يَهْدِىٓ إِلَى ٱلْحَقِّ ۚ قُلِ ٱللَّهُ يَهْدِى لِلْحَقِّ ۗ أَفَمَن يَهْدِىٓ إِلَى ٱلْحَقِّ أَحَقُّ أَن يُتَّبَعَ أَمَّن لَّا يَهِدِّىٓ إِلَّآ أَن يُهْدَىٰ ۖ فَمَا لَكُمْ كَيْفَ تَحْكُمُونَ (۳٥)
Say, ‘Is there anyone among your partners who may guide to the truth?’ Say, ‘Allah guides to the truth. Is He who guides to the truth worthier to be followed, or he who is not guided unless shown the way? What is the matter with you? How do you judge?’ (35)

Key Points

The previous Ayah referred to the Origin (Mabda'), the Resurrection (Ma'ad), and the creation of existence, while this Ayah refers to prophethood (Nubuwwah), Imamate, and the matter of guiding humanity. Guidance (Hidayah) is of two types: one is showing the way, and the other is leading to the destination and goal. The first type is the work of the prophets, «جعلناهم ائمةً یهدون بأمرنا» (255). However, guidance in the second sense is exclusive to Allah. «انّك لاتهدى من اَحبَبتَ ولكن اللَّه یهدى» (256). «یهدى الى الحقّ» and «یهدى للحقّ» both carry the same meaning (257). According to continuously narrated (mutawatir) traditions from both Shia and Ahl al-Sunnah, the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «الحقّ مع علىٍّ و علىٌّ مع الحقّ» (The truth is with Ali, and Ali is with the truth); the truth is always with Ali علیه السلام and Ali علیه السلام is with the truth (258).

255) Al-Anbiya, 73. 256) Al-Qasas, 56. 257) Tafsir al-Mizan. 258) Ihqaq al-Haqq, vol. 5, pp. 28 and 635.

Messages

1- Guidance (Irshad and Hidayah) is one of the divine prerogatives of Allah. «قل اللَّه یهدى» (The criterion for worship is the ability to guide).

2- Correct guidance requires extensive, error-free knowledge, power, love, and purpose. Therefore, no one can be a guide (Hadi) except Allah and those whom He has appointed as guides. How can idols and tyrants (Taghuts) be guides? (259) «قل اللّه یهدى للحقّ»

3- The one who guides to the truth is worthy of being followed. «احقّ ان یتّبع»

4- The principle of prophethood (Nubuwwah), Imamate, and obedience to the infallible leadership is a rational principle. «فما لكم كیف تحكمون»

259) One who has not received existence from the Bestower of Existence, how can he become a Bestower of Existence?

Ayah

وَمَا يَتَّبِعُ أَكْثَرُهُمْ إِلَّا ظَنًّا ۚ إِنَّ ٱلظَّنَّ لَا يُغْنِى مِنَ ٱلْحَقِّ شَيْـًٔا ۚ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلِيمٌۢ بِمَا يَفْعَلُونَ (۳٦)
Most of them just follow conjecture; indeed conjecture is no substitute for the truth. Indeed Allah knows best what they do. (36)

Key Points

That which possesses true value is "knowledge" (‘ilm). Strong conjecture and assurance also hold value if they have a correct basis, such as the conjecture obtained by a jurist (faqih) from the apparent meaning of verses (Ayat) and narrations (Riwayat) regarding the ruling of Allah (especially in subsidiary issues). However, conjecture concerning belief (aqidah) is worthless if its source is the imitation of ancestors and ignorant prejudices.

Messages

1- The majority is not always a sign of truthfulness. «و ما یتّبع اكثرهم الا ظناً»

2- The opponents of the prophets lack logic. «الاّ ظّناً»

3- Polytheism (Shirk) has no scholarly basis. «هل من شركائكم... و ما یتّبع الا ظناً»

4- In beliefs and perspectives, one must seek knowledge, not conjecture. «انّ الظّنَّ لایغنى من الحقّ»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ هَـٰذَا ٱلْقُرْءَانُ أَن يُفْتَرَىٰ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَـٰكِن تَصْدِيقَ ٱلَّذِى بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ وَتَفْصِيلَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَا رَيْبَ فِيهِ مِن رَّبِّ ٱلْعَـٰلَمِينَ (۳۷)
This Quran could not have been fabricated by anyone besides Allah; rather, it is a confirmation of what was [revealed] before it, and an elaboration of the Book, there is no doubt in it, from the Lord of all the worlds. (37)

Messages

1- The comprehensiveness of the Quran and its content is of such a nature that fabrication and attribution of it to other than Allah is inapplicable. «ان یفترى»

2- Heavenly Books confirm one another. The difference lies in the conciseness and detail of their content. «تصدیق الّذى بین یدیه»

3- The Quran is a means of growth and nurturing. «هذا القرآن... من ربّ العالمین»

Ayah

أَمْ يَقُولُونَ ٱفْتَرَىٰهُ ۖ قُلْ فَأْتُوا۟ بِسُورَةٍ مِّثْلِهِۦ وَٱدْعُوا۟ مَنِ ٱسْتَطَعْتُم مِّن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (۳۸)
Do they say, ‘He has fabricated it?’ Say, ‘Then bring a surah like it, and invoke whomever you can, besides Allah, should you be truthful.’ (38)

Key Points

The Quran has challenged and called for a contest several times regarding the miraculous nature of the Divine Word and the inability of jinn and humans to produce its like, or even an Ayah similar to it, including in Ayah 13 of Surah Hud. Furthermore, despite the fact that millions of opponents have been and are active over centuries to weaken the Quran and defeat Islam, this challenging call of the Quran has remained unanswered until now and will remain unanswered until the Day of Judgment.

Distinctions of the Quran: Since the discussion concerns the challenge of the Quran, we point to some distinctions of this eternal Divine miracle:

1- Embedding profound knowledge in short phrases: For example, concerning men and women, He expresses it as, «هنّ لباس لكم و انتم لباسٌ لهنّ» (260) [They are clothing for you and you are clothing for them]. To express the weakness of non-Divine powers, He likens them to the house of the spider. (261) Or, He declares them incapable of creating a single fly. «لن یخلقوا ذباباً» (262) 2- Sweetness of speech and penetration: Even if read a thousand times, it does not become stale; rather, a new point is gained every time. 3- Rhythm and music of the speech: The resonance and rhythm of its words are unique, and if an Ayah of the Quran is found among the speech of any Arabic speaker, or among the narrations (Hadith), it is clearly distinguishable. 4- Comprehensiveness of the Quran: It encompasses everything from proof (Burhan) to parable (Mathal), from this world to the Hereafter, as well as family, legal, political, military, ethical, historical issues, and more. 5- Realism: Its content is not based on speculation or conjecture. Even its stories are documented and real. 6- Universal and global: People benefit from it regardless of their level or location, and the Quran is not a specialized book. 7- Eternal: The more human life and sciences advance, the more secrets of the Quran are discovered. 8- Increasing growth: Despite having the most enemies and enduring numerous attacks, it has experienced the greatest growth throughout its existence. 9- A miracle in hand: This miracle is accessible to everyone; it is of the type of speech and word, which is available to all. 10- It is both a miracle and a book of instruction and law. 11- It originates from an unlettered individual (the Prophet) and in a region deprived of literacy. 12- Nothing has been added to it or subtracted from it, and it is immune from distortion (tahrif).

260) Baqarah, 187. 261) Ankabut, 41. 262) Hajj, 73.

Messages

1- To prove its miraculous nature, the Quran proposes the simplest method, which is inviting people to produce a Surah like it. «فأتوا بسورة مثله»

2- Not only the entire Quran, but even a single Surah from it is a miracle. «بسورةٍ»

3- In issuing the challenge, the Quran has even offered a concession, being satisfied with the production of just one Surah like the Quran. «بسورةٍ»

4- The challenge of the Quran is not specific to a certain time or place; this request remains open until the Day of Judgment, until the end of human life, and everywhere on earth. «و ادعوا من استطعتم من دون اللّه»

5- The challenge of the Quran is not limited to the common people; rather, it challenges all jurists, literary experts, geniuses, and others. «و ادعوا من استطعتم من دون اللّه»

6- In this challenge, He has not specified particular resources or personnel. «و ادعوا من استطعتم من دون اللّه»

7- In this challenge, He has also provided motivation (or provocation). «ان كنتم صادقین»

Ayah

بَلْ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِمَا لَمْ يُحِيطُوا۟ بِعِلْمِهِۦ وَلَمَّا يَأْتِهِمْ تَأْوِيلُهُۥ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ كَذَّبَ ٱلَّذِينَ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۳۹)
Indeed, they deny something whose knowledge they do not comprehend, and whose explanation has not yet come to them. Those who were before them denied likewise. So observe how was the fate of the wrongdoers! (39)

Key Points

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: We learn two great lessons from two verses of the Qur'an: A: Do not speak until we have knowledge. «الم یؤخذ علیهم میثاق الكتاب ان لا یقولوا على اللَّه الاّ الحقّ» (263) B: Do not reject anything until we have knowledge. «بل كذبّوا بما لم یحیطوا بعلمه» (This very verse.) (264)

Abdul Azim Hasani narrates from Imam Jawad عليه السلام that Imam Ali عليه السلام said: I stated four points for which Allah revealed verses: 1- «المرء مخبوء تحت لسانه» (A person's character is hidden beneath his tongue.) The verse was revealed: «ولتعرفنّهم فى لحن القول» (265) meaning, you will recognize them by their manner of speech. 2- I said: «مَن جهل شیئاً عاداه» (Whoever is ignorant of something becomes hostile towards it.) The verse was revealed: «بل كذّبوا بما لم یحیطوا» 3- I said: «قیمة كلّ امرء ما یحسنه» (The worth of every person is what he excels at/does well.) The verse was revealed: «انّ اللّه اصطفاه علیكم و زاده بسطة فى العلم و الجسم» (266) meaning, Allah chose Talut because of his capabilities. 4- «القتل یقلّ القتل» (Killing the oppressor prevents the increase of killing.) The verse was revealed: «و لكم فى القصاص حیاة» (267)

263) Al-A'raf, 169. 264) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan. 265) Muhammad, 30. 266) Al-Baqarah, 247. 267) Al-Baqarah 179; Kafi, Vol. 1, p. 166.

Messages

1- The opponents rejected the Qur'an without having comprehensive knowledge of it. «كذّبوا بما لم یحیطوا»

2- We should not reject something merely because of ignorance. «كذّبوا بما لم یحیطوا»

3- General and superficial knowledge has little effect. What causes enlightenment is "comprehensive knowledge" (scientific mastery). «لم یحیطوا بعلمه»

4- Throughout history, the opposition of the enemies of the prophets has often been based on ignorance. «كذلك كذّب الذین من قبلهم»

5- Awareness of the history and fate of the opponents of the prophets is necessary. «فانظر كیف كان عاقبة...»

Ayah

وَمِنْهُم مَّن يُؤْمِنُ بِهِۦ وَمِنْهُم مَّن لَّا يُؤْمِنُ بِهِۦ ۚ وَرَبُّكَ أَعْلَمُ بِٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (٤٠)
Some of them believe in it, and some of them do not believe in it, and your Lord best knows the agents of corruption. (40)

Messages

1- Man possesses free will, and is not compelled (to believe). «منهم من یؤمن به و منهم من لایؤمن به»

2- A leader should not expect all people to believe in his path and religion. «ومنهم من لایؤمن به»

3- Disbelief and corruption are inseparable. Both sin prevents faith, and disbelief becomes a motive for corruption. «لایؤمن به...المفسدین»

4- Man is under the supervision of the Lord. «ربّك اعلم بالمفسدین»

Ayah

وَإِن كَذَّبُوكَ فَقُل لِّى عَمَلِى وَلَكُمْ عَمَلُكُمْ ۖ أَنتُم بَرِيٓـُٔونَ مِمَّآ أَعْمَلُ وَأَنَا۠ بَرِىٓءٌ مِّمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ (٤۱)
If they deny you, say, ‘My deeds belong to me and your deeds belong to you: you are absolved of what I do and I am absolved of what you do.’ (41)

Messages

1- People cannot be forced to accept faith. The duty of the prophets is instruction and guidance, not compulsion. «وان كذبوبك فقل...»

2- The leader must prepare himself for the rejection by a group of people. «وان كذّبوك»

3- We should not feel defeated in the face of deniers, because we receive the reward for guidance and instruction, and the opponents will also face the punishment for their denial and obstinacy, but we are the winners of this transaction. «لى عملى و لكم عملكم»

4- The Prophet took a decisive stance against the deniers. «فقل لى عملى و لكم عملكم»

5- The profit and loss of every person returns only to himself. «لى عملى ولكم عملكم» (The words «لى» and «لكم» precede «عملى» and «عملكم»)

6- Islam is a religion of reasoning and ethics, not compromise with the disbelievers. If they do not accept the truth, we should declare our dissociation from their behavior and beliefs. «انتم بریئون، وانا برى»

7- Being pleased with the actions of others makes a person a partner in the reward or punishment. «انتم بریئون، وانا برى» (The reason why the recompense of every person is specific to himself is that neither you nor I are pleased with the actions of the other)

Ayah

وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَسْتَمِعُونَ إِلَيْكَ ۚ أَفَأَنتَ تُسْمِعُ ٱلصُّمَّ وَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ (٤۲)
There are some of them who prick up their ears at you. But can you make the deaf hear even if they do not exercise their reason? (42)

Messages

1- Not all listeners have good intentions. Sometimes the obstinate ones listen repeatedly to find excuses and pretexts. «منهم من یستمعون الیك...»

2- The spiritual sickness of a person's heart sometimes reaches a point where even the words of the Prophet do not cure him. «افانت...تسمع الصمّ»

3- For the guidance of people, readiness for acceptance is necessary; clear proofs and a worthy leader alone are not sufficient. «لایعقلون»

4- Hearing (whether direct or indirect) is not important; what is important in guidance is reasoning and acceptance; otherwise, even an animal hears. «یستمعون , لایعقلون»

Ayahs

وَمِنْهُم مَّن يَنظُرُ إِلَيْكَ ۚ أَفَأَنتَ تَهْدِى ٱلْعُمْىَ وَلَوْ كَانُوا۟ لَا يُبْصِرُونَ (٤۳)
There are some of them who observe you. But can you guide the blind even if they do not perceive? (43)
إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يَظْلِمُ ٱلنَّاسَ شَيْـًٔا وَلَـٰكِنَّ ٱلنَّاسَ أَنفُسَهُمْ يَظْلِمُونَ (٤٤)
Indeed Allah does not wrong people in the least; rather, it is people who wrong themselves. (44)

Key Points

A group will be resurrected blind on the Day of Judgment, and this is the result of their spiritual blindness (blindness of the heart) and forgetting the divine signs (Ayat) in this world. Such people are as if they are blind: «و نحشرهم یوم القیامة اعمى ... كذلك اتتك آیاتنا فنسیتها وكذلك الیوم تُنسى » (268) Imam Hadi عليه السلام said: Whoever assumes that Allah compels His servants to sin and then punishes them has attributed injustice to Allah and has denied this Ayah (44). (269)

268) Taha, 124-126. 269) Bihar, Vol. 5, p. 71.

Messages

1- Not every gaze brings insight, and not every observer is drawn to the truth. «ینظر، لایبصرون»

2- Even the Prophets are not effective among the spiritually blind. «افانت تهدى العمى»

3- Those who do not reach the truth through observation are like the blind. «ینظر، لایبصرون»

4- Allah has sent the best school of thought, the best Book, and the best leader for the guidance of people; if they do not accept them, they have wronged themselves. «إِنَّ اللَّهَ لَا یَظْلِمُ النَّاسَ...أَنفُسَهُمْ یَظْلِمُونَ»

Ayah

وَيَوْمَ يَحْشُرُهُمْ كَأَن لَّمْ يَلْبَثُوٓا۟ إِلَّا سَاعَةً مِّنَ ٱلنَّهَارِ يَتَعَارَفُونَ بَيْنَهُمْ ۚ قَدْ خَسِرَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِلِقَآءِ ٱللَّهِ وَمَا كَانُوا۟ مُهْتَدِينَ (٤٥)
On the day He will gather them [it will be] as if they had not remained [in the world] except for an hour of the day getting acquainted with one another. They are certainly losers who deny the encounter with Allah, and they are not guided. (45)

Key Points

The grandeur of the Day of Judgment is such that the life preceding it appears very short. This reality is also mentioned in other verses, including: «لم یلبثوا الاّ عشیّةً او ضُحاها» [270] (It is) as if they had not remained (in the world or Barzakh) for more than an evening or its morning. «و تظّنون ان لبثتم الاّ قلیلاً» [271] You will think that you had not remained (before the Day of Judgment) except for a short period. «ان لبثتم الاّ عشراً» [272] You will think that you had not resided (there) for more than ten days. «لبثنا یوماً او بعض یومٍ» [273] You will think that you had remained in the world or Barzakh for a day or part of a day. «ما لبثوا غیرساعةً» [274] You will think that you had not remained for more than an hour. The shortness of the worldly life is either due to its fleeting nature, or because of the failure to utilize time and opportunity, or in comparison to the life of the Hereafter, or because humans in Barzakh (the intermediate realm) are seemingly asleep and upon waking feel that the time was brief. In any case, both the enjoyment (of the world) is little compared to the Hereafter, and its time is little, as if it were no more than an hour.

270) An-Nazi'at, 46. 271) Al-Isra, 52. 272) Taha, 103. 273) Al-Mu'minun, 112. 274) Ar-Rum, 54.

Messages

1- Worldly life is very brief compared to the Day of Resurrection. «ساعةً من النهار»

2- On the Day of Judgment, people will recognize one another. «یتعارفون بینهم»

3- The true losers are those who, by denying the Day of Judgment, are deprived of the eternal benefits of the Hereafter and are content only with worldly pleasures. Whereas the believers enjoy divine blessings both in this world and have a share in the Hereafter. «قد خسر الّذین كذّبوا»

Ayah

وَإِمَّا نُرِيَنَّكَ بَعْضَ ٱلَّذِى نَعِدُهُمْ أَوْ نَتَوَفَّيَنَّكَ فَإِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ ثُمَّ ٱللَّهُ شَهِيدٌ عَلَىٰ مَا يَفْعَلُونَ (٤٦)
Whether We show you a part of what We promise them, or take you away [before that], [in any case] their return will be to Us and Allah will be witness to what they do. (46)

Key Points

The punishment of some disbelievers occurred during the battles of Badr, Hunayn, al-Ahzab (the Confederates), and the Conquest of Mecca. Even after the passing of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, the Hand of Allah remains open for retribution. The delay in the punishment of the polytheists led to two issues: one, the concern of the Prophet, and the other, the fault-finding of the polytheists who would ask, "Where is the promised punishment?" (which we will read about in the next two verses). This verse explains the wisdom behind the delay in punishment, offering solace to the Prophet and the people: that the return of all is to Us, and We are witnesses to their deeds, so the polytheists should not be pleased with their excuses. Imam Ali عليه السلام said: «اتّقوا معاصى اللّه فى الخلوات فانّ الشاهد هو الحاكم» "Avoid disobeying Allah even in private and solitude, for the Witness is Himself the Judge." [275]

275) Bihar al-Anwar, Vol. 73, p. 364.

Messages

1- Allah will settle the account of the disbelievers and punish them, whether now or later. Therefore, Muslims should not fall into despair or worry due to the delay in divine decree. «وامّا نرینّك...او نتوفینّك فالینا مرجعهم»

2- The passing of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله does not affect Allah's judgment regarding the disbelievers. «اونتوفّینّك»

3- The return of all is to God, and no one has the power to escape the court of Divine Justice. «الینا مرجعهم»

4- Allah is a witness to our deeds. «اللّه شهیدٌ»

Ayahs

وَلِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَّسُولٌ ۖ فَإِذَا جَآءَ رَسُولُهُمْ قُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْقِسْطِ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (٤۷)
There is an apostle for every nation; so when their apostle comes, judgement is made between them with justice, and they are not wronged. (47)
وَيَقُولُونَ مَتَىٰ هَـٰذَا ٱلْوَعْدُ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (٤۸)
They say, ‘When will this promise be fulfilled, should you be truthful?’ (48)

Key Points

The disbelievers repeatedly asked the prophets, mockingly, "If you are truthful, when will the time of Allah's wrath be?!" This contemptuous question was a way of strengthening the resolve of those who were influenced by them. But is knowing the time of an event a prerequisite for its occurrence?!

Question: If every nation has a messenger (rasūl), then why has prophethood (risālah) ceased? Answer: The intent of rasūl is not the person of the Prophet, but rather His message and mission (risālah), whether direct or indirect. Otherwise, there would have to be a prophet in every region!

The presence of the prophets among the people and their just judgment relates both to this world and possibly to the Hereafter, which is also mentioned in Ayah 69 of Sūrat Az-Zumar.

Imam Baqir عليه السلام said: The divine prophets, "يَقْضُونَ بِالْقِسْطِ" (they judge with justice and equity). [276]

276) Tafsīr Sāfī.

Messages

1- It is among the divine traditions that no nation shall be without a prophet. "لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَسُولٌ"

2- The history of religion is concurrent with the history of humanity. [277] "لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَسُولٌ"

3- Divine wrath (which was mentioned in the previous Ayah) occurs after the completion of the proof (iṭmām al-ḥujjah) and the coming of the prophets. "لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ رَسُولٌ"

4- The spread of equity and justice in society is due to the mission (bi‘thah) of the prophets. "إِذَا جَاءَ رَسُولُهُمْ قُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ بِالْقِسْطِ"

5- On the Day of Judgment, every group will appear with their own prophet, and then judgment will be passed among them with justice. [278] "جَاءَ رَسُولُهُمْ قُضِيَ..."

277) As we read in another Ayah: "وَإِن مِّنْ أُمَّةٍ إِلَّا خَلَا فِيهَا نَذِيرٌ" (Fāṭir, 24).

278) Another Ayah states: "وَجِيءَ بِالنَّبِيِّينَ وَالشُّهَدَاءِ وَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُم بِالْحَقِّ" (Az-Zumar, 69).

Ayah

قُل لَّآ أَمْلِكُ لِنَفْسِى ضَرًّا وَلَا نَفْعًا إِلَّا مَا شَآءَ ٱللَّهُ ۗ لِكُلِّ أُمَّةٍ أَجَلٌ ۚ إِذَا جَآءَ أَجَلُهُمْ فَلَا يَسْتَـْٔخِرُونَ سَاعَةً ۖ وَلَا يَسْتَقْدِمُونَ (٤۹)
Say, ‘I have no control over any benefit for myself nor [over] any harm except what Allah may wish. There is a time for every nation: when their time comes, they shall not defer it by a single hour nor shall they advance it.’ (49)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, there was a "Messenger" (رسول) for every nation (ummah); here, there is a "term" (أجل) for every nation. Perhaps it can be said that the arrival of the time of wretchedness or felicity for every people depends on following the Messenger or ignoring him.

Question: If every nation has a term (ajal), then the Islamic nation must also have a term and an end? Answer: The term "nation" (ummah) means a group or community, not a religion (madhhab). Therefore, it does not contradict the finality of prophethood (Khātamiyyah) and is not evidence for the necessity of changing the religion at every period of time.

Messages

1- No one, not even the Prophets, possesses power or ownership independently. «لااملك لنفسى»

2- The power of the Prophets and the Awliyā (saints) is a reflection of Divine power. [279] «الاّ ما شاء اللَّه»

3- The sign of the Prophet's صلى الله عليه وآله sincerity is His saying: «لااملك لنفسى نفعاً ولا ضرّا» (I possess no power for myself, neither benefit nor harm).

4- Determining the time of punishment for the disbelievers belongs solely to Allah. «متى هذا الوعد... قل لااملك...»

5- Society, like individuals, has a term (ajal), felicity, wretchedness, growth, and deviation. «لكل امّةٍ أجل» (For every nation there is a term). (The expiration of terms is sometimes through destruction and annihilation, and sometimes through the loss of glory and honor.)

6- Worldly government and power are not eternal; let us not be deceived by them. «لكل امّةٍ أجل»

7- Granting respite to nations is among the Divine traditions (Sunan). «لكلّ امّةٍ أجل»

8- The destinies of nations are based on Divine traditions and admit neither advancement nor delay. Therefore, let us not hasten Divine decrees. «لایستأخرون...»

279) Unfortunately, in Tafsir al-Maraghi, a detailed discussion has been presented to reject tawassul (seeking intercession), relying on the phrase «قل لا املك» (Say: I possess no power), while neglecting the phrase «الاّ ما شاء اللَّه» (Except what Allah wills)!!

Ayahs
