قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِنْ أَتَىٰكُمْ عَذَابُهُۥ بَيَـٰتًا أَوْ نَهَارًا مَّاذَا يَسْتَعْجِلُ مِنْهُ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ (٥٠)
Say, ‘Tell me, should His punishment overtake you by night or day, [you will not be able to avert it]; so what part of it do the guilty seek to hasten?’ (50)
أَثُمَّ إِذَا مَا وَقَعَ ءَامَنتُم بِهِۦٓ ۚ ءَآلْـَٔـٰنَ وَقَدْ كُنتُم بِهِۦ تَسْتَعْجِلُونَ (٥۱)
‘What! Do you believe it when it has befallen? Now? While you would seek to hasten it [earlier]?!’ (51)

Key Points

We read in a hadith that the manifestation of the punishment mentioned in the Ayah is the punishment that will befall the people of the Qiblah (Muslims) at the End of Time (Ākhir al-Zamān). [280]

280) Tafsir Noor al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Divine wrath is sudden, and the time of day or night is irrelevant to it. «بیاتاً او نهاراً»

2- The wise person must exercise caution when punishment is probable, not haste (in demanding it). «ماذا یستعجل»

3- When Allah's wrath surprises the criminals, «بیاتاً او نهاراً», the path to salvation is closed. «ماذا یستعجل»

4- Believing at the moment of danger has no effect. «الآن...»

5- The polytheists have always sought the acceleration of divine punishment. «كنتم تستعجلون»

Ayah

ثُمَّ قِيلَ لِلَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا۟ ذُوقُوا۟ عَذَابَ ٱلْخُلْدِ هَلْ تُجْزَوْنَ إِلَّا بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَكْسِبُونَ (٥۲)
Then it will be said to those who were wrongdoers, ‘Taste the everlasting punishment. Shall you not be requited for what you used to earn?’ (52)

Key Points

The punishment ('adhāb) mentioned in the previous Ayah referred to divine retribution in this world, while in this Ayah, the punishment of the Resurrection is discussed. Therefore, He said: «ذوقوا» (taste!) and did not say: «یذوقون» (they taste).

Messages

1- Opponents of the Prophets are oppressors (wrongdoers). «ظلموا» (Considering the previous Ayahs)

2- Divine retribution is eternal for a group of criminals. «عذاب الخلد»

3- The punishments of the Hereafter are both spiritual and physical. «قیل... ذوقوا»

4- Punishments are according to Divine justice. «هل تجزون الاّ بما كنتم تكسبون»

5- Man is free in performing good and bad deeds, not compelled. «تكسبون»

Ayah

۞ وَيَسْتَنۢبِـُٔونَكَ أَحَقٌّ هُوَ ۖ قُلْ إِى وَرَبِّىٓ إِنَّهُۥ لَحَقٌّ ۖ وَمَآ أَنتُم بِمُعْجِزِينَ (٥۳)
They inquire of you, ‘Is it true?’ Say, ‘Yes! By my Lord, it is true, and you cannot frustrate [Him].’ (53)

Key Points

This Ayah is similar to Ayah 8 of Surah At-Tur: «انّ عذابَ ربّك لواقعٌ ما له من دافعٍ» Certainly, the divine punishment will occur, and no barrier can prevent it.

Messages

1- Allah's promises are definite and certain. «اى و ربّى انّه لحقّ» (The oath, the word inna, and the letter lam in «لحقّ» are signs of emphasis.)

2- The leader must remove doubt and ambiguity from the people. «قل»

3- In the face of doubts, one must speak with decisiveness. «اى و ربّى»

4- Sometimes, an oath is necessary to remove the people's doubt and instability. «اى و ربّى انّه لحقّ»

5- In the system of existence, no power can prevent the Divine Will. «ما انتم له بمعجزین»

Ayah

وَلَوْ أَنَّ لِكُلِّ نَفْسٍ ظَلَمَتْ مَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ لَٱفْتَدَتْ بِهِۦ ۗ وَأَسَرُّوا۟ ٱلنَّدَامَةَ لَمَّا رَأَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ۖ وَقُضِىَ بَيْنَهُم بِٱلْقِسْطِ ۚ وَهُمْ لَا يُظْلَمُونَ (٥٤)
Were any soul that has done wrong to possess whatever there is on the earth, it would surely offer it for ransom. They will hide their remorse when they sight the punishment; and judgement will be made between them with justice and they will not be wronged. (54)

Key Points

| Giving ransom (fidyah) to escape punishment has also been mentioned in other verses. Including the verse, "ما فى الارض جمیعاً و مثله معه لافتدوا به" [281] (even if they had all that is in the earth and the like of it with it, they would offer it as ransom). Even if they give twice what is in the earth, they will not be saved from Allah's punishment. Likewise, in Surah Al-Ma'arij, He says: The criminal would wish on that Day to ransom himself with all his children, his spouse, his brother, his relatives, and all that is on the earth, so that he may be saved, but it will not be accepted. [282]

281) Ra'd, 18 and Zumar, 47. 282) Ma'arij, 11 - 15.

Messages

1- On the Day of Judgment, there is no way of salvation for the criminal, and no person or wealth will be sacrificed for another. "و لو..."

2- Self-love (Hubb Nafs) is the strongest instinct in man. Because he is willing to sacrifice all existence for his own salvation. "ما فى الارض لافتدت به"

3- Criminals conceal their regret in their hearts so that they do not face gloating (or reproach). [283] "اسرّوا النّدامة"

4- On the Day of Judgment, some reveal their regret by saying, "If only..." (I wish...), some bite their hands, and others conceal it through silence and suppression. "اسرّوا النّدامة"

5- The leaders of polytheism (shirk) conceal their regret on the Day of Judgment so that they do not feel ashamed before their subordinates. [284] "اسرّوا النّدامة"

6- In the court of Divine Justice, neither power is effective, "ما انتم بمعجزین" (you are not able to frustrate Him), nor is wealth. "ما فى الأرض..."

7- In this world, it is possible for someone to be wronged due to a judge's mistake, but on the Day of Judgment, there is no error, and no one will be wronged. "قضى بینهم بالقسط وهم لایظلمون"

8- We should not treat even the oppressor (ẓālim) with oppression. "ظلمت , لایظلمون"

283) Tafsir Safi.

284) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Ayahs

أَلَآ إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ أَلَآ إِنَّ وَعْدَ ٱللَّهِ حَقٌّ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (٥٥)
Look! To Allah indeed belongs whatever is in the heavens and the earth. Look! Allah’s promise is indeed true; but most of them do not know. (55)
هُوَ يُحْىِۦ وَيُمِيتُ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ (٥٦)
It is He who gives life and brings death, and to Him you shall be brought back. (56)

Key Points

|

Messages

1- Knowing is not enough; remembrance and attention to one's knowledge and beliefs are important. «اَلا»

2- Since Allah is the Owner of all existence, the disbelievers possess nothing of their own to offer as ransom for their salvation. (Considering the previous Ayah)

3- His ownership over existence is a sign of His power to fulfill His promises. «للّه ما فى السموات... انّ وعد الله حقّ»

4- The very One to Whom existence belongs is able to give life and cause death. «للّه ما فى السموات... یحیى و یمیت»

5- Life, death, and the ultimate outcome of all affairs are in the hand of Allah and towards Him is the return. «یحیى و یمیت و الیه ترجعون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ قَدْ جَآءَتْكُم مَّوْعِظَةٌ مِّن رَّبِّكُمْ وَشِفَآءٌ لِّمَا فِى ٱلصُّدُورِ وَهُدًى وَرَحْمَةٌ لِّلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (٥۷)
O mankind! There has certainly come to you an advice from your Lord, and a cure for what is in the breasts, and a guidance and mercy for the faithful. (57)

Key Points

Maw‘izah (admonition/exhortation) is a restraint mixed with warning and caution. (285) Shifā’un limā fī ṣ-ṣudūr (a healing for what is in the breasts) means the source of purification for the soul and heart from spiritual afflictions. This is because spiritual diseases are harder than physical diseases, and the art of the Quran lies precisely in this healing of spiritual ailments. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: "When tribulations confuse you like the dark night, then hold fast to the Quran, for it is an intercessor whose intercession is accepted..." In the onslaught of dark and terrifying tribulations, seek refuge in the Quran, for it is both an admonition, a healing, a light, and a mercy. (286) Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: "A healing for what is in the breasts, from the diseases of thoughts and ambiguous matters." The Quran is a healing for all spiritual diseases, ambiguities, and thoughts. (287) It seems the Ayah refers to the four stages of training and perfection, namely: A: The stage of admonition, concerning outward actions. Maw‘iẓatun min Rabbikum (an admonition from your Lord). B: The stage of purifying the soul from vices. Shifā’un limā fī ṣ-ṣudūr (a healing for what is in the breasts). C: The stage of finding the way toward the goal. Hudā (guidance). D: The stage of receiving Divine Mercy. Raḥmatun lil-mu’minīn (a mercy for the believers).

285) Mufradāt Rāghib. 286) Bihār, Vol. 92, p. 17. 287) Tafsīr Nūr ath-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The revelation of a Book that includes admonition, healing, guidance, and mercy is among the affairs of Divine Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). Min Rabbikum (from your Lord).

2- The Quran is the cure for every ailment. The remedy for pains must be sought from the school of the Quran, not the schools of the East or the West. Shifā’un limā fī ṣ-ṣudūr (a healing for what is in the breasts).

3- The Quran is an admonition for all people, but only the truth-accepting group is included in Divine guidance and mercy. Yā ayyuhā n-nāsu... hudāw wa raḥmatun lil-mu’minīn (O mankind... guidance and a mercy for the believers).

Ayah

قُلْ بِفَضْلِ ٱللَّهِ وَبِرَحْمَتِهِۦ فَبِذَٰلِكَ فَلْيَفْرَحُوا۟ هُوَ خَيْرٌ مِّمَّا يَجْمَعُونَ (٥۸)
Say, ‘In Allah’s grace and His mercy—let them rejoice in that! It is better than what they amass.’ (58)

Key Points

Considering that the Qur'an is a healing (shifa), an admonition (maw'izah), and a mercy (rahmah), as mentioned in the previous Ayah, the apparent meaning of this Ayah is that the intent behind grace (فضل) and mercy (رحمت) is adherence to the Qur'an itself.

According to Imam Ali عليه السلام: On the Day of Judgment, it will be proclaimed that every farmer will suffer from a blight, except those who sowed in the field of the Qur'an. Therefore, sow in the land of the Qur'an. «فكونوا من حرثة القرآن» (288)

The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: «مَن هداه اللَّه للأسلام وعلّمه القرآن ثمّ شكى الفاقة كتب اللَّه الفاقة بین عینیه الى یوم القیامة» (Whoever Allah guides to Islam and teaches the Qur'an, yet he complains of poverty, Allah writes poverty between his eyes until the Day of Resurrection.) Whoever Allah honored with the blessing of Islam and the understanding of the Qur'an, but he feels miserable, is truly miserable, and the seal of wretchedness will be placed upon his forehead until the Day of Resurrection. Then He recited this Ayah. (289)

And in another narration, He said: Turn to the Qur'an so that your life may be the life of the blessed and your death the death of the martyrs, and so that you may be saved from the regret of the Day of Judgment, because the Qur'an is the Word of Allah, the Most Compassionate (al-Rahman), a barrier and a safeguard against the evil of Satan, and the cause of superiority and the heaviness of the Scale (of deeds). (290)

In narrations, grace (فضل) and mercy (رحمت) have been interpreted as Prophethood (Nubuwwah) and Imamate (Imamah). (291) Just as grace (فضل) has been interpreted as the general blessings of Allah and mercy (رحمت) as the specific blessings of Allah. (292)

The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The people of faith should know that the blessing of religion is better than all the wealth of the disbelievers, and they should rejoice in it. (293)

(288) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 17. (289) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. (290) Bihar, Vol. 89, p. 19. (291) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. (292) Tafsir al-Mizan. (293) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- We should be proud of a law like the Qur'an, which is a healing, an admonition, a guidance, and a mercy, and which is superior to all human laws. «فلیفرحوا»

2- We should rejoice in the blessing of Wilayah (Guardianship/Leadership), not in wealth or status. «فلیفرحوا»

3- We should consider blessings to be from the grace of Allah, not from ourselves, so that our joy takes on a divine color and does not lead to boasting or arrogance. (294) «بفضل اللّه...فلیفرحوا»

4- The only blessing whose possession should cause joy is the Qur'an. «بفضل اللّه و برحمته... فلیفرحوا» (The precedence of «بفضل اللّه وبرحمته» indicates exclusivity.)

5- Material blessings sometimes cause temptation (fitnah) and corruption and are fleeting, but spiritual blessings are lasting and lead to growth. «خَیرٌ ممّا یَجمَعون»

6- Spiritual blessings are superior to all material blessings. «خیرٌممّایَجمعون» (Whoever benefits from the Qur'an should not be saddened by his own poverty or the wealth of others.)

(294) Tafsir Kabir of Fakhr Razi.

Ayah

قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُم مَّآ أَنزَلَ ٱللَّهُ لَكُم مِّن رِّزْقٍ فَجَعَلْتُم مِّنْهُ حَرَامًا وَحَلَـٰلًا قُلْ ءَآللَّهُ أَذِنَ لَكُمْ ۖ أَمْ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَفْتَرُونَ (٥۹)
Say, ‘Have you regarded what Allah has sent down for you of [His] provision, whereupon you have made some of it unlawful and [some] lawful?’ Say, ‘Did Allah give you the sanction [to do so], or do you fabricate a lie against Allah?’ (59)

Key Points

Examples of the baseless prohibitions imposed by the polytheists are mentioned in Ayah 103 of Surah Al-Ma'idah: «ما جَعَل اللَّه من بحیرةٍ ولاسائبةٍ ولا وصیلةٍ ولاحامٍ» They would allocate a share of their agriculture to idols and temples and forbid it upon themselves, or they would consider what was in the wombs of animals lawful for men and unlawful for women: «وقالوا ما فى بطون هذه الأنعام خالصةً لذكورنا و محرّم على ازواجنا» (295) However, the Quran considers all of this to be slander against Allah and illegitimate. «على اللّه تفترون»

295) Al-An'am, 139.

Messages

1- The fundamental and general rule regarding edibles is that they are lawful (halal), unless their prohibition (hurmah) is proven through revelation. «ما انزل اللّه لكم»

2- Sustenance (rizq) is sent down by Allah. «انزل اللَّه لكم من رزق»

3- In matters of Sharia rulings, one must not precede the Lawgiver (Allah); just as one cannot make what Allah has forbidden lawful, one also cannot deem what is lawful as forbidden. «فجعلتم منه حراماً وحلالاً»

4- Legislation (tashri') and lawmaking belong exclusively to Allah. «آللَّه اذن لكم»

5- Those who possess "legislative authority" (Wilayah Tashri'i) can make things lawful or unlawful with Divine permission. «اللَّه اذن لكم»

6- Establishing an innovative law (bid'ah) and attributing it to Allah is a crime. «جعلتم منه حراماً و حلالاً ، على اللَّه تفترون»

7- The Quran condemns false customs and traditions that have no root in revelation or the permission of Allah. «على اللّه تفترون»

Ayah

وَمَا ظَنُّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَذُو فَضْلٍ عَلَى ٱلنَّاسِ وَلَـٰكِنَّ أَكْثَرَهُمْ لَا يَشْكُرُونَ (٦٠)
What is the idea of those who fabricate lies against Allah [concerning their situation] on the Day of Resurrection? Indeed Allah is gracious to mankind, but most of them do not give thanks. (60)

Key Points

The Prophet of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله criticized a man who possessed wealth and means but lived in a disheveled state, asking why he did not attend to his appearance. He said: Manifest the divine blessing, for Allah loves that the effects of His bounty be seen upon His servant in the most beautiful manner. "فان اللّه یحبّ ان یُرى اثره على عبده حسناً" (296)

296) Tafsir Maraghi.

Messages

1- Slandering Allah incurs punishment. We should not be deceived by the respite Allah grants in this world, nor should we neglect the court of the Day of Judgment. "وما ظنّ الذین..."

2- Prohibiting lawful divine blessings results in both worldly deprivation and shame and punishment on the Day of Judgment. "ما ظنّ الّذین یفترون...یوم القیامة"

3- In benefiting from divine blessings, neither excess (extravagance) nor deficiency (neglect) should be practiced. Neither should one drown in desires and materialism, nor should one abstain from blessings and deprive oneself of lawful enjoyments. "اللّه لذو فضل على الناس"

4- Blessings are the favor (فضل) of Allah, not our due right; therefore, we must be grateful. "اللّه لذو فضل... لایشكرون"

5- Prohibiting lawful things granted by Allah is a form of ingratitude. "لایشكرون"

Ayah

وَمَا تَكُونُ فِى شَأْنٍ وَمَا تَتْلُوا۟ مِنْهُ مِن قُرْءَانٍ وَلَا تَعْمَلُونَ مِنْ عَمَلٍ إِلَّا كُنَّا عَلَيْكُمْ شُهُودًا إِذْ تُفِيضُونَ فِيهِ ۚ وَمَا يَعْزُبُ عَن رَّبِّكَ مِن مِّثْقَالِ ذَرَّةٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَلَا فِى ٱلسَّمَآءِ وَلَآ أَصْغَرَ مِن ذَٰلِكَ وَلَآ أَكْبَرَ إِلَّا فِى كِتَـٰبٍ مُّبِينٍ (٦۱)
You do not engage in any work, neither do you recite any part of the Quran, nor do you perform any deed without Our being witness over you when you are engaged therein. Not an atom’s weight escapes your Lord in the earth or in the sky, nor [is there] anything smaller than that nor bigger, but it is in a manifest Book. (61)

Key Points

The "Kitab Mubin" (Clear Book) is the Preserved Tablet (Lawh Mahfuz) or the vast knowledge of Allah concerning all beings in existence, which encompasses both the deeds of man and small and large things in the earth and the heavens. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Whenever the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) recited this Ayah, He would weep intensely. [297]

297) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- All the actions of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله are important because He is a model and an exemplar (Uswah). (The expression "in any matter" (شَأْنٍ) indicates the importance of the action.) «ما تكون فى شأن»

2- Even the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله is under observation. «وما تكون...الا كنّا علیكم شهوداً»

3- When reciting the Quran, one must be careful not to read it incorrectly, because in addition to the negative consequences, witnesses are observing our work. «ماتتلوا...الا كنّا علیكم شهوداً»

4- Allah is a Witness over man's thoughts («شَأْنٍ»), his speech («تَتْلُو»), and his deeds. « لا تعملون من عملٍ الا كنّا علیكم شهودا»

5- The recitation of the Quran is placed alongside the status of prophethood and all the deeds of the people. (In this Ayah, Allah's witnessing is mentioned regarding three things: all the affairs of the Prophet, all the deeds of the people, and the recitation of the Quran; this expression indicates the importance of reciting the Quran.)

6- The world is the presence of Allah, and He is a Witness over our deeds; thus, the respite He grants us is not a sign of His negligence or lack of encompassment. «كنّا علیكم شهوداً»

7- Both Allah and the angels are witnesses over human deeds. «كنّا علیكم شهوداً»

8- Attention to the presence of Allah leads to shame and modesty before Him. «شهوداً»

9- Allah's knowledge is uniform regarding the far and the near, the small and the large, the apparent and the hidden. «مثقال ذرّة , فى الارض, فى السماء, اصغر , اكبر»

Ayahs

أَلَآ إِنَّ أَوْلِيَآءَ ٱللَّهِ لَا خَوْفٌ عَلَيْهِمْ وَلَا هُمْ يَحْزَنُونَ (٦۲)
Look! The friends of Allah will indeed have no fear nor will they grieve (62)
ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ يَتَّقُونَ (٦۳)
—those who have faith and are Godwary. (63)

Key Points

These verses recount the characteristics of the friends (Awliya') of Allah. Their motto is: «انّا نخاف من ربّنا...» [298] The result of this fear (khawf) is piety (taqwa), and the fruit of piety is: «لایحزنهم الفزع الأكبر» [299] This lack of fear for the friends of Allah is both in this world and the Hereafter. «لاخوف علیهم» The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: The silence of the friends of Allah is remembrance (dhikr), their gaze is a lesson (ibrah), their speech is wisdom (hikmah), and their movement in society is a source of blessing (barakah). [300] Ali عليه السلام said: Allah has hidden His friend (Wali) among the people, so never belittle any Muslim, for perhaps he is one of the friends of Allah. «انّ اللَّه اخفى ولیّه فى عباده فلاتستصغرنّ عبداً من عبیداللَّهِ فربّما یكونُ ولیّه وانتَ لاتعلم» [301]

298) Al-Insan, 10. 299) Al-Anbiya, 103. 300) Tafsir Safi. 301) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- Whoever is a friend (Wali) of Allah, Allah is also His friend. [302] «انّ اولیاء اللّه لاخوف علیهم»

2- Whoever possesses divine fear (khawf), will have no other fear. «ان اولیاء اللّه لاخوف علیهم» (He whose account is clean has no fear of reckoning.)

3- The friends of Allah have a bright future, free from fear and sorrow. «انّ اولیاء اللّه لاخوف علیهم و لا هم یحزنون»

4- Faith (Iman) and piety (taqwa) are the deep-rooted characteristics of the friends of Allah. «آمنوا وكانوا یتّقون»

5- Perpetual faith and piety both remove anxiety and make a person one of the friends of Allah. «انّ اولیاء اللّه... آمنوا وكانوا یتّقون»

302) Ayahs 257 of Al-Baqarah «اللَّه ولىّ الذین آمنوا», 68 of Aal-i-Imran «واللَّه ولىّ المؤمنین» and 19 of Al-Jathiyah «واللَّه ولىّ المتقین»

Ayah

لَهُمُ ٱلْبُشْرَىٰ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَفِى ٱلْـَٔاخِرَةِ ۚ لَا تَبْدِيلَ لِكَلِمَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ ذَٰلِكَ هُوَ ٱلْفَوْزُ ٱلْعَظِيمُ (٦٤)
For them is good news in the life of this world and in the Hereafter. (There is no altering the words of Allah.) That is the great success. (64)

Key Points

The glad tidings (Bishara) for the people of faith and piety in this world are conveyed through true dreams and visions (rū'yāhā-ye ṣādiqah), Divine inspirations, the descent of tranquility (sakīnah) and peace, the neutralization of conspiracies, the answering of prayers, and meeting the Infallibles (Ma'sūmīn) at the time of death. In the Hereafter, it is the reception of Divine blessings. Just as the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله said regarding this Ayah: The worldly glad tidings are the good dreams that contain good news, and the glad tidings of the Hereafter are the tidings given to him at the moment of death that you and those who carry you to the grave shall be forgiven. [303] And in a narration, Imam Baqir عليه السلام said to Abu Hamzah al-Thumali: At the moment of death, Muhammad and Ali علیهما السلام will be present before the believer and will say: Come towards us, for your future is better than your past, and your soul shall join the Spirit of Allah. [304]

The glad tidings of Allah to His friends (Awliyā') in this world are mentioned in other verses of the Quran, which shows that the friends of Allah are successful and victorious even in this world, because:

1- They have tranquility. «أَلا بذكر اللَّه تطمئنّ القلوب» [305] 2- They have reliance (Tawakkul). «فلیتوكّل المؤمنون» [306] 3- They receive unseen aid. «وجنوداً لم تروها» [307] 4- They are always victorious. «اِحدَى الحُسنیین» [308] 5- They have insight and light. «یجعل لكم فرقانا» [309] 6- They do not reach a dead end. «یجعل له مَخرجاً» [310] 7- They do not see their work as futile. «فمن یعمل مثقال ذرّة خیراً یره» [311] 8- They are not afraid of blame. «لایخافون لَومَة لائم» [312] 9- They are not afraid of people. «انّ الناس قد جمعوا لكم فاخشوهم فزادهم ایمانا» [313] 10- They are not afraid of the tyrant (Tāghūt). «فاقض ما انتَ قاض» [314] 11- They are not afflicted by confusion (Hayrah). «یهدیهم ربّهم بایمانهم» [315] 12- They have popularity/love. «سیجعل لهم الرّحمن وُدّا» [316] 13- They make their transient deeds eternal with the Divine coloring. «صِبغة اللَّه» [317]

303) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 304) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 305) Ra'd, 28. 306) Aal-i-Imran, 122. 307) Ahzab, 9. 308) Tawbah, 52. 309) Anfal, 29. 310) Talaq, 2. 311) Zilzal, 7. 312) Ma'idah, 54. 313) Aal-i-Imran, 173. 314) Taha, 72. 315) Yunus, 9. 316) Maryam, 96. 317) Baqarah, 138.

Messages

1- Only the friends of Allah (Awliyā') possess both this world and the Hereafter. «لهم البشرى فى الحیوةالدنیا...والاخرة»

2- Divine promises are definite and certain. «لهم البشرى ... لاتبدیل...»

3- The great success (Fawz al-'Aẓīm) belongs to those who have consistently maintained faith and piety. «آمنوا و كانوا یتّقون... الفوز العظیم»

Ayah

وَلَا يَحْزُنكَ قَوْلُهُمْ ۘ إِنَّ ٱلْعِزَّةَ لِلَّهِ جَمِيعًا ۚ هُوَ ٱلسَّمِيعُ ٱلْعَلِيمُ (٦٥)
Do not grieve at their remarks; indeed all might belongs to Allah; He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (65)

Key Points

The disbelievers attributed the titles of poet, soothsayer (kāhin), sorcerer (sāhir), and madman (majnūn) to the Prophet. In addition to these, they sometimes said: He has no distinction over us; rather, he is a human being just like us. «ان انتم الا بشر مثلنا» [318] Sometimes they said: His words were fabricated and prepared by another group and dictated to him. «اعانه علیه قوم آخرون» [319] Sometimes they said: If we wished, we could certainly produce the like of this Qur'an. «لو نشاء لقلنا مثل هذا» [320] And sometimes they said: His words are tales of the ancients. «اساطیر الاوّلین» [321] And regarding his followers, they said: They are lowly individuals. «ما نراك اتّبعك الا الذین هم اراذِلُنا» [322] But the will of Allah is that, in the face of all that humiliation, the Prophet and the believers should be honored (azīz). 'Izzah (honor/might) means impenetrability. This is true for Allah, for the Prophet, for the believers «فللّه العزّة ولرسوله و للمؤمنین» [323], and for the school of thought (the religion) «وانّه لكتابٌ عزیز» [324].

318) Ibrahim, 10. 319) Furqan, 4. 320) Anfal, 31. 321) Nahl, 24. 322) Hud, 27. 323) Munafiqun, 8. 324) Fussilat, 41.

Messages

1- The goal of enemy propaganda is to humiliate the religion and the Islamic Ummah, but Allah consoles the Prophet and prevents the disbelievers from achieving this goal. «لایحزنك قولهم»

2- A believer should not be affected by the poisoned propaganda of the disbelievers or feel weak. «لایحزنك قولهم»

3- Allah is the supporter of His chosen servants (Awliyā') and has guaranteed the victory of the religion and the Prophets. [325] «لایحزنك قولهم انّ العزّة للّه»

4- Honor ('Izzah) is in the hand of Allah and cannot be broken by the sayings of this person or that. «انّ العزّة للّه»

325) كتب اللَّه لأغلبَنَّ انا و رسلى انّ اللَّه قوىّ عزیز» Mujadilah, 21.

Ayah

أَلَآ إِنَّ لِلَّهِ مَن فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۗ وَمَا يَتَّبِعُ ٱلَّذِينَ يَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ شُرَكَآءَ ۚ إِن يَتَّبِعُونَ إِلَّا ٱلظَّنَّ وَإِنْ هُمْ إِلَّا يَخْرُصُونَ (٦٦)
Look! To Allah indeed belongs whoever is in the heavens and whoever is on the earth. What do they pursue who invoke partners besides Allah? They merely follow conjectures and they just make surmises. (66)

Key Points

It is possible that the phrase "وما یتّبع..." is a question and "ان یتّبعون" is its answer. Meaning: What do those who turn to imaginary partners besides Allah follow? They follow nothing but conjecture. It is also possible that the particle "ما" (mā) is a coordinate to "مَن" (man), meaning that even what the polytheists consider partners to Allah are owned by Him.

Messages

1- The absolute ownership (sovereignty) of Allah is the proof of His Oneness (Tawhid) and His complete Glory/Might (Izzah). «انّ العزة للّه جمیعاً... ان للّه مَن فى السموات»

2- Everything is the possession of Allah; how can you set a possession (the owned) as a partner to the Owner? «للّه من فى السموات و من فى الارض»

3- There are intelligent beings in the heavens. ("مَن" [man] refers to a conscious being.)

4- Polytheists lack logic and a scientific basis. «ان یتّبعون الّا الظّنّ»

5- Polytheists follow their own fantasies and delusions, not divine partners, because Allah has no partner. Therefore, they lie. «ان هم الّا یخرصون»

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى جَعَلَ لَكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ لِتَسْكُنُوا۟ فِيهِ وَٱلنَّهَارَ مُبْصِرًا ۚ إِنَّ فِى ذَٰلِكَ لَـَٔايَـٰتٍ لِّقَوْمٍ يَسْمَعُونَ (٦۷)
It is He who made the night for you, that you may rest in it, and the day to provide visibility. There are indeed signs in that for people who listen. (67)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the ownership (sovereignty) of Allah was mentioned, and in this Ayah, His wise management of the cosmic order is referred to. Spiritual tranquility is discussed in the Quran under titles such as: recitation, night vigil, glorification, supplication, prostration, and staying awake at night. [326] But physical tranquility is achieved through sleep and rest. «منامكم باللیل والنّهار» [327]

326) Surahs: Aal-i Imran, 113; Al-Isra, 79; Taha, 130; Az-Zumar, 9; and Al-Muzzammil, 2. 327) Ar-Rum, 23.

Messages

1- The creation of night and day is managed, wisely planned, and aimed at benefiting humanity. «جعل... لتسكنوا»

2- Night is for tranquility, whether spiritual tranquility or physical tranquility. «لتسكنوا فیه»

Ayah

قَالُوا۟ ٱتَّخَذَ ٱللَّهُ وَلَدًا ۗ سُبْحَـٰنَهُۥ ۖ هُوَ ٱلْغَنِىُّ ۖ لَهُۥ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ إِنْ عِندَكُم مِّن سُلْطَـٰنٍۭ بِهَـٰذَآ ۚ أَتَقُولُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا تَعْلَمُونَ (٦۸)
They say, ‘Allah has taken a son!’ Immaculate is He! He is the All-sufficient. To Him belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is in the earth. You have no authority for this [statement]. Do you attribute to Allah what you do not know? (68)

Key Points

«اتّخاذ ولد» (Ittikhādh walad), means adopting someone as a child. This is different from natural birth (tawallud) and having children. The polytheists considered the angels to be the daughters of Allah, and the Jews adopted 'Uzayr (Ezra), and the Christians adopted 'Isa (Jesus) as sons of Allah. It is mentioned in the Quran that the Aziz (Minister) of Egypt told his wife Zulaikha: Let us adopt Yusuf (Joseph) as a son, «نتّخذه ولداً» [328]. And Asiyah, the wife of Pharaoh, told him: Let us adopt Musa (Moses) as a son. «لاتقتلوه عسى ان ینفعنا او نتّخذه ولداً» [329]

328) Yusuf, 21. 329) Qasas, 8.

Messages

1- Allah neither fears loneliness that He would need to adopt a child for companionship, nor does He desire an heir that He would adopt a child, nor does He need an assistant that He would take a helper, nor does He need a child for the continuation of His kind. «سبحانه هو الغنى»

2- Religious beliefs must be accompanied by proof and reasoning. «ان عندكم من سلطان»

3- Speech without proof and without knowledge holds no value. «ان عندكم من سلطان... ما لاتعلمون»

Ayahs

قُلْ إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ يَفْتَرُونَ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ٱلْكَذِبَ لَا يُفْلِحُونَ (٦۹)
Say, ‘Indeed those who fabricate lies against Allah will not be felicitous.’ (69)
مَتَـٰعٌ فِى ٱلدُّنْيَا ثُمَّ إِلَيْنَا مَرْجِعُهُمْ ثُمَّ نُذِيقُهُمُ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلشَّدِيدَ بِمَا كَانُوا۟ يَكْفُرُونَ (۷٠)
An enjoyment in this world; then to Us shall be their return, then We shall make them taste the severe punishment because of what they used to defy. (70)

Key Points

| "Matā'" (مَتاع) and "Mut'ah" (مُتعه) refer to short-term utilization or enjoyment. Since man's utilization of the world and its blessings is short, the Quran uses the term "matā'" for worldly affairs. Question: If those who fabricate lies do not attain prosperity, why do we see them enjoying greater comfort in their material lives? Answer: This comfort and enjoyment is temporary, "مَتاعٌ فِی الدُّنیا" (a brief enjoyment in the world), but their main punishment is in the Hereafter, at the time when they return to Him.

Messages

1- Those who consider certain individuals to be the children of Allah, hoping for intercession, will not achieve their goal. "قالوا اتّخذوا اللّه ولداً...لایفلحون"

2- The liar does not attain prosperity. "الذین یفترون... لایفلحون"

3- Temporary enjoyment is worthless if it is followed by eternal punishment. "مَتاعٌ فِی الدُّنیا , نُذِیقُهُمُ العَذابَ الشَّدِیدَ"

4- Worldly pleasures are fleeting. "مَتاعٌ فِی الدُّنیا"

5- Remembering the Resurrection (Ma'ad) is one of the factors that prevents deviation and sin. "إلَینا مَرجِعُهُم"

6- Fabricating lies against Allah through various types of innovations (bid'ah), distortions, interpretations based on personal opinion (tafsir bi al-ra'y), and attributing a son to Him, is disbelief (kufr). "بِما کانُوا یَکفُرُونَ"

Ayah

۞ وَٱتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ نُوحٍ إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِۦ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِن كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكُم مَّقَامِى وَتَذْكِيرِى بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ فَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ فَأَجْمِعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَكُمْ وَشُرَكَآءَكُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُنْ أَمْرُكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ غُمَّةً ثُمَّ ٱقْضُوٓا۟ إِلَىَّ وَلَا تُنظِرُونِ (۷۱)
Relate to them the account of Noah when he said to his people, ‘O my people! If my stay [among you] be hard on you and [also] my reminding you of Allah’s signs, [for my part] I have put my trust in Allah. So conspire together, along with your partners, leaving nothing vague in your plan, then carry it out against me without giving me any respite. (71)

Key Points

This Ayah conveys the steadfastness and reliance of Prophet Noah (عليه السلام) on Divine aid, showing that he had no fear of the enemies' threats and plots. It is narrated that Imam Husayn (عليه السلام) also recited this Ayah to the army of Kufa in Karbala, before the noon of Ashura. [330]

330) Mawsu'at Kalimat al-Imam.

Messages

1- Acquainting people with important and beneficial news of the past, its history, and its philosophy, is among the duties of Divine leaders. «واتل علیهم نبأ»

2- The Prophets were from the people and with the people, and they maintained an emotional approach toward their opponents even until the last moment. «یا قومِ»

3- If the heart lacks readiness, even the admonition and reminder of the Prophets is burdensome upon it. «كَبُر علیكم مقامى و تذكیرى»

4- Faith in the goal is the greatest leverage for the Prophets' resistance and their challenge to fight. «ان كان كبر علیكم مقامى...فاجمعوا امركم»

5- The Prophets challenged their opponents to a fight. «اجمعوا امركم»

6- Relying on Allah, the Prophets belittled opposing powers, gave courage to the believers, and considered all powers futile. [331] «اجمعوا امركم... ثم اقضوا»

7- Correct assessment, mobilization of forces, and decisive resolution are among the principles of struggle. «امركم و شركائكم ثم لا یكن امركم علیكم غمّة»

8- The Prophets were not afraid of martyrdom. «ثم اقضوا الىّ ولاتنظرون»

331) The recitation of these verses by the Muslims, who were a minority in Mecca, demonstrates the high spirit and complete courage of the Prophet.

Ayah

فَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَمَا سَأَلْتُكُم مِّنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ (۷۲)
If you turn your back [on me], I do not ask any reward from you; my reward lies only with Allah and I have been commanded to be of those who submit [to Allah].’ (72)

Key Points

Worries are often either about life or wealth. In the previous Ayah, the discussion concerned the Prophets having no fear of the enemies' plots regarding their lives. In this Ayah, their lack of expectation regarding wealth and reward from the people is addressed.

Messages

1- The people's acceptance or rejection of the Prophets' invitation has no material effect on the Prophets. «ما سألتكم من اجر»

2- The successful and effective preacher is one who does not covet people's wealth and announces this to the people. «ما سألتكم من اجر»

3- The Prophets sought no reward whatsoever from the people—neither wealth, nor services, nor the like. «ما سألتكم من اجر»

4- If all people turn away from the religion, it has no effect on the divine leader; rather, he is submissive to the religion of Allah. «فان تولّیتم، اكون من المسلمین»

5- Whoever believes in the reward of the Hereafter easily detaches his heart from worldly rewards. «اِن اجرى الا على اللَّه»

6- The mission of all the Prophets is submission to the Truth. «من المسلمین»

7- Whoever wishes to compel people toward submission to Allah must himself be submissive. «اُمرت... من المسلمین»

8- Although people turn away today, Allah does not lack submissive servants and true believers throughout history; you should join them. «من المسلمین»

Ayah

فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ وَأَغْرَقْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ (۷۳)
But they impugned him. So We delivered him and those who were with him in the ark and We made them the successors, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. So observe how was the fate of those who were warned! (73)

Key Points

The companions of Noah were eighty people, while the disbelievers were numerous. However, through Divine aid, the disbelievers were destroyed, and the believers became the inheritors of the earth. (332) This verse refers to the good outcome for the believers and the destruction of those who opposed the call of the prophets.

(332) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

1- Mere faith is not enough; accompanying and aligning oneself with the Divine leaders is necessary to achieve salvation. (This is indicated by the phrase) "من معه" (those who were with him) instead of "آمنوا به" (those who believed in him).

2- The result of faith and steadfastness is salvation and governance. ("نجیّنا" - We saved, "جعلناهم خلائف" - We made them successors/inheritors).

3- Ultimately, victory belongs to the people of faith, and disgrace belongs to the people of disbelief (kufr) and polytheism (shirk). ("نجیّنا... اغرقنا...")

4- It is among the Divine traditions (Sunan) to first grant respite and then inflict wrath and punishment upon the rebellious. «اغرقنا الذین كذّبوا»

5- The result of insistence upon disbelief and going astray is destruction. «اغرقنا الذین كذّبوا»

6- First, the completion of the argument (proof), warning, and admonition, then wrath and punishment. «اغرقنا... عاقبة المنذرین»

7- Beneficial and constructive history is that which encourages the proponents of truth and threatens the followers of falsehood. «فانظر كیف كان عاقبة المنذرین»

8- Understanding history and the factors leading to the progress or extinction of nations is necessary for guiding movements. «فانظر كیف كان عاقبة المنذرین»

Ayah

ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ رُسُلًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِمْ فَجَآءُوهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَمَا كَانُوا۟ لِيُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِمَا كَذَّبُوا۟ بِهِۦ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَطْبَعُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُعْتَدِينَ (۷٤)
Then after him We sent [other] apostles to their people. They brought them manifest proofs, but they would not believe something they had denied before. Thus do We seal the hearts of the transgressors. (74)

Messages

1- Sending Prophets successively has been a Divine tradition. «بعثنا من بعده رسلاً» (Allah sent Prophets in the time interval between Prophet Noah and Moses.)

2- All Prophets possessed miracles. «فجاؤهم بالبینات»

3- All Prophets had opponents. «فما كانوا لیؤمنوا»

4- Man is free; even after seeing miracles and hearing of Divine wrath, he still disobeys. «فما كانوا لیؤمنوا»

5- Arrogance and transgression are the grounds for sealing hearts. «نطبع على قلوب المعتدین»

6- Those who do not believe despite seeing miracles are transgressors. «كذّبوا، المعتدین»

Ayah

ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِم مُّوسَىٰ وَهَـٰرُونَ إِلَىٰ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِۦ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا فَٱسْتَكْبَرُوا۟ وَكَانُوا۟ قَوْمًا مُّجْرِمِينَ (۷٥)
Then, after them, We sent Moses and Aaron to Pharaoh and his elite with Our signs, but they acted arrogantly and they were a guilty lot. (75)

Key Points

The meaning of al-mala' (مَلأ) refers to prominent, eye-catching figures, such as the palace dwellers and courtiers who were considered Pharaoh's entourage. Pharaoh had divided society into two groups: the Coptic palace dwellers and the deprived Israelites (Sibti), enslaving one group while the other group consisted of the palace dwellers. «وجعل اهلها شیعاً یستضعف طائفةً منهم» [333] Pharaoh's great crimes were his claim to divinity, «أنا ربّكم الأعلى» [334], killing innocent children, and keeping the girls alive for servitude. «سنقّتل ابنائهم ونستحیى نساءهم» [335]

333) Al-Qasas, 4. 334) An-Nazi'at, 24. 335) Al-A'raf, 124.

Messages

1- Struggling against the tyrant (Taghut) is the first duty of the prophets, because fighting the cause of corruption is more important than fighting its effect. «فرعون و ملائه»

2- Where the social structure was tribal and ethnic, the prophets went to their people; but where there was an established organization and system, they went to the roots (of power). [336] «الى فرعون و ملائه»

3- Tyrants (Taghuts) derive their power from their supporters and entourage; otherwise, they are hollow from within. «وملائه»

4- The prophets possessed miracles. «بعثنا... بایاتنا»

5- Pharaohs and tyrants have always stood against the prophets. «بعثنا... فاستكبروا»

6- Those who become accustomed to sin do not easily accept the path of the prophets. «كانواقوماً مجرمین»

336) The Quran uses the phrase «الى قومهم» (to their people) regarding a group of prophets, but concerning Prophet Moses, it states «اذهب الى فرعون» (Go to Pharaoh).

Ayah

فَلَمَّا جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ مِنْ عِندِنَا قَالُوٓا۟ إِنَّ هَـٰذَا لَسِحْرٌ مُّبِينٌ (۷٦)
When the truth from Us came to them, they said, ‘This is indeed plain magic!’ (76)

Messages

1- People do not seek out the truth; rather, the proponents of truth must approach the people. «جاءهم الحقّ»

2- Both the Essence of Allah is Truth, and so are the prophets, the divine law, the miracle, and everything attributed to Him. «جائهم الحقّ»

3- The Prophets have a direct relationship with Allah, and their miracles and programs originate from Him. «عندنا»

4- The preacher and leader must know that there will always be a group in society that opposes their programs. «قالوا...»

5- The accusation of sorcery is the sharpest, simplest, and most prevalent accusation leveled against the Prophets. «لسحرٌ»

6- The source of the accusation is the rebellious nature of man. «كانوا... مجرمین... ان هذا لسحر...»

7- Opponents use the strongest emphasis in their opposition. [337] «انّ هذا لسحرمبین»

337) The nominal sentence, the particle inna (إِنّ), and the particle lām (لَ) convey this emphasis.

Ayah

قَالَ مُوسَىٰٓ أَتَقُولُونَ لِلْحَقِّ لَمَّا جَآءَكُمْ ۖ أَسِحْرٌ هَـٰذَا وَلَا يُفْلِحُ ٱلسَّـٰحِرُونَ (۷۷)
Moses said, ‘Do you say of the truth when it comes to you [that it is magic]? Is this magic? Magicians do not find salvation.’ (77)

Key Points

Usually, sorcery is the work of corrupt individuals seeking fame and fortune. Therefore, the enemies of the Prophets used this accusation to humiliate them before the people. It is necessary to mention that sorcery (magic) is considered forbidden (haram) in jurisprudence (fiqh). Just as Imam Khomeini قدس سره considered the act of sorcery (sihr), teaching and learning it, earning income through it, and also conjuring (sleight of hand) where a person, through trickery, makes the unreal appear real in the eyes of the viewers, [forbidden] [338].

338) Tahrir al-Wasilah, Vol. 1, p. 498.

Messages

1- Moses announced his victory and the defeat of those who leveled accusations. «قال موسى...»

2- The enemies introduced the truth (al-haqq) as sorcery and the Prophets as sorcerers. «اتقولون للحقّ... سحر هذا»

3- A sorcerer will not prosper (be successful) because of deceiving people, using illegitimate means, and seeking fame. «لایفلح»

Ayah

قَالُوٓا۟ أَجِئْتَنَا لِتَلْفِتَنَا عَمَّا وَجَدْنَا عَلَيْهِ ءَابَآءَنَا وَتَكُونَ لَكُمَا ٱلْكِبْرِيَآءُ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَمَا نَحْنُ لَكُمَا بِمُؤْمِنِينَ (۷۸)
They said, ‘Have you come to us to turn us away from what we found our fathers following, so that supremacy may be yours in the land? We will not believe in the two of you.’ (78)

Key Points

Respect for ancestors and kindness towards parents is separate from absolute submission to them. The culture of predecessors should not be accepted without logic and reason, nor should one hold undue fanaticism towards it.

Messages

1- Cultural revolutions and intellectual transformations have always had repercussions in society. «لتَلفِتَنا عما وجدنا»

2- The actions of ancestors are influential in later generations. «وجدنا علیه آباءنا»

3- The foundation of the Pharaonic government was the superstitious beliefs of the people's ancestors. «وجدناعلیه آباءنا»

4- Sometimes, respect for ancestors and preserving ancient heritage is a tool for resisting the movement of the prophets. «آباءنا»

5- Imitation of ancestors without logic and reason is invalid. «وجدنا علیه آباءنا»

6- In the name of preserving cultural heritage, not every wrong action is permissible. «وجدنا علیه اباؤنا»

7- Because tyrants (Taghuts) seek power, they portray the reformative efforts of reformers as 'seeking power' (or 'seeking dominion'). «تكون لكما الكبریاء»

8- Reformers should prepare themselves to hear accusations from the oppressors. «تكون لكماالكبریاء»

Ayahs

وَقَالَ فِرْعَوْنُ ٱئْتُونِى بِكُلِّ سَـٰحِرٍ عَلِيمٍ (۷۹)
Pharaoh said, ‘Bring me every expert magician.’ (79)
فَلَمَّا جَآءَ ٱلسَّحَرَةُ قَالَ لَهُم مُّوسَىٰٓ أَلْقُوا۟ مَآ أَنتُم مُّلْقُونَ (۸٠)
So when the magicians came, Moses said to them, ‘Throw down what you have to throw.’ (80)

Key Points

Pharaoh wanted to neutralize the divine movement of Moses (عليه السلام) with the help of skilled magicians. The magicians possessed tools such as ropes, sticks, etc., which, when coated with specific substances, would appear to move under the sun.

Messages

  1. Tyrants (Taghuts) try to stop the movement of the prophets through clamor, commotion, and propaganda. «ائتونى بكل ساحر»
  2. Tyrants utilize all their power to suppress opponents. «ائتونى بكلّ ساحرٍ علیم»
  3. Tyrants sometimes buy off scholars and specialists and use them to achieve their goals. «جاء السحرة»
  4. Challenging the opposition is one of the characteristics of the prophets. «القوا»
  5. Prophets speak decisively because they are confident in their goal and in divine assistance. «القوا ما انتم ملقون»

Ayah

فَلَمَّآ أَلْقَوْا۟ قَالَ مُوسَىٰ مَا جِئْتُم بِهِ ٱلسِّحْرُ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ سَيُبْطِلُهُۥٓ ۖ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ لَا يُصْلِحُ عَمَلَ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (۸۱)
So when they threw down [their sticks and ropes], Moses said, ‘What you have produced is magic. Indeed Allah will bring it to naught presently. Indeed Allah does not foster the efforts of those who cause corruption. (81)

Messages

1- Although falsehood may have a temporary sway, it is ultimately destined to vanish. «سیبطله»

2- Allah reforms the affairs of the believers: «وَاصلَحَ بالَهم» (339) but He does not reform the work of the corruptors. «لایصلح عمل المفسدین»

3- The sorcerer is a corruptor (mufsid) and sorcery is corruption (fasad). Therefore, it must be confronted. «عمل المفسدین»

(339) Muhammad, 2.

Ayah

وَيُحِقُّ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْحَقَّ بِكَلِمَـٰتِهِۦ وَلَوْ كَرِهَ ٱلْمُجْرِمُونَ (۸۲)
Allah will confirm the truth with His words, though the guilty should be averse.’ (82)

Key Points

In the context of the Quran, the term Kalimah (Word) is applied to the laws, traditions (Sunan), and everything that Allah makes the means for the realization of His Will.

Messages

1- Realizing and making the truth victorious is among the promises and Divine traditions (Sunan). «و یحقّ اللَّه الحق»

2- Establishing the truth requires tools and means. «بكلماته»

3- The desire of the criminals (or oppressors) to prevent the victory of the truth is ineffective. «ولوكره المجرمون»

Ayah

فَمَآ ءَامَنَ لِمُوسَىٰٓ إِلَّا ذُرِّيَّةٌ مِّن قَوْمِهِۦ عَلَىٰ خَوْفٍ مِّن فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَإِي۟هِمْ أَن يَفْتِنَهُمْ ۚ وَإِنَّ فِرْعَوْنَ لَعَالٍ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَإِنَّهُۥ لَمِنَ ٱلْمُسْرِفِينَ (۸۳)
But none believed in Moses except some youths from among his people, for the fear of Pharaoh and his elite that he would persecute them. For Pharaoh was indeed a tyrant in the land, and indeed he was an unrestrained [despot]. (83)

Key Points

In the Qur'an, we sometimes read: «آمن به» (Amana bihi), sometimes «آمن معه» (Amana ma'ahu), and sometimes «آمن له» (Amana lahu). Each possesses a distinct characteristic: «آمن به» signifies faith (Iman), while «آمن له» signifies submission (Taslim).

Messages

1- Faith must be accompanied by submission and obedience. «آمن لموسى»

2- The ground for intellectual transformation and inclination toward the truth is greater among youth, because they have pure hearts and their attachments to wealth and status are fewer. «آمن...ذرّیة من قومه»

3- The Prophet of Islam was not the only one who endured hardships in inviting people and witnessed individuals failing to believe. «فما آمن لموسى»

4- Sometimes the elders go astray while the youth attain the truth. «الاّ ذریّة من قومه»

5- It seems the believing youth feared not only Pharaoh but also the leaders of their own people, who were conservative, fearful, and dependent. «ملائهم» (their chiefs), not «ملائه» (his chiefs).

6- The Pharaonic system cannot prevent inner faith. «آمن ، على خوفٍ»

7- Firm faith is never weakened by a bad environment, a corrupt system, unhealthy economics and propaganda, or an atmosphere of terror. «آمن... على خوف»

8- In the Pharaonic system, there is suppression and suffocation. «على خوف من فرعون»

9- The torturing system is the Pharaonic system. «یفتنهم»

10- The root of human corruption lies in his desire for superiority. «ان فرعون لعالٍ فى الأرض»

11- The extravagant system is the Pharaonic system. «لمن المسرفین»

Ayah

وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِن كُنتُمْ ءَامَنتُم بِٱللَّهِ فَعَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلُوٓا۟ إِن كُنتُم مُّسْلِمِينَ (۸٤)
And Moses said, ‘O my people! If you have faith in Allah, put your trust in Him, if you have submitted [to Him].’ (84)

Messages

1- The leader must give morale to the nation (Ummah). «وقال موسى یا قوم»

2- Faith (Iman) is inseparable from reliance (Tawakkul). «ان كنتم آمنتم باللّه فعلیه توكلوا»

3- Submission (Taslim) to Divine pleasure is higher than faith (Iman). [340] «ان كنتم مسلمین» follows «ان كنتم آمنتم».

4- Difficulties must be overcome through reliance (Tawakkul) and submission (Taslim). «توكلوا ان كنتم مسلمین»

340) It is possible that the intent of faith (Iman) is faith in Allah, and the intent of submission (Taslim) is obedience to Moses.

Ayahs

فَقَالُوا۟ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْنَا رَبَّنَا لَا تَجْعَلْنَا فِتْنَةً لِّلْقَوْمِ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۸٥)
Whereat they said, ‘In Allah we have put our trust.’ ‘Our Lord! Do not make us a [means of] test for the wrongdoing lot, (85)
وَنَجِّنَا بِرَحْمَتِكَ مِنَ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلْكَـٰفِرِينَ (۸٦)
and deliver us by Your mercy from the faithless lot.’ (86)

Key Points

It is possible that the intent of al-Zālimīn (the wrongdoers/oppressors) refers to the elders of the Children of Israel who obeyed Pharaoh for the sake of benefit and comfort, and the intent of al-Kāfirīn (the disbelievers) refers to the supporters of Pharaoh. Therefore, the request for believing progeny is protection from the evil of internal and external opponents. Whoever relies upon Allah, Allah both loves him, "إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الْمُتَوَكِّلِينَ" (Indeed, Allah loves those who rely [upon Him]) [341], and provides for him. "وَمَن يَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى اللَّهِ فَهُوَ حَسْبُهُ" (And whoever relies upon Allah, then He is sufficient for him) [342].

341) Āl ‘Imrān, 159. 342) Al-Ṭalāq, 3.

Messages

1- First reliance (Tawakkul), then supplication (Du'a). "تَوَكَّلْنَا رَبَّنَا" (We have relied, our Lord).

2- Through faith, reliance, and supplication, we save ourselves from the evil of the oppressors. "تَوَكَّلْنَا رَبَّنَا نَجِّنَا" (We have relied, our Lord, save us).

3- The Pharaohites are extravagant (musrifūn) [343], oppressive (ẓālimūn), and disbelieving (kāfirūn). Extravagance paves the way for oppression, and oppression, in turn, paves the way for disbelief. "الْمُسْرِفِينَ الظَّالِمِينَ الْكَافِرِينَ" (The extravagant, the oppressors, the disbelievers).

4- One must first pray for the preservation of the ideological identity, "لَا تَجْعَلْنَا فِتْنَةً" (Do not make us a trial), and then for personal safety. "نَجِّنَا" (Save us). Because if the oppressors prevail, the path of oppression and disbelief will dominate, and the believers will be humiliated and afflicted.

343) With reference to Ayah 83.

Ayah

وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰ وَأَخِيهِ أَن تَبَوَّءَا لِقَوْمِكُمَا بِمِصْرَ بُيُوتًا وَٱجْعَلُوا۟ بُيُوتَكُمْ قِبْلَةً وَأَقِيمُوا۟ ٱلصَّلَوٰةَ ۗ وَبَشِّرِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۸۷)
We revealed to Moses and his brother [saying], ‘Settle your people in the city, and let your houses face each other, and maintain the prayer, and give good news to the faithful.’ (87)

Key Points

«قبلة» (Qiblah) means both "opposite" or "facing one another," and also "the direction of the Ka'bah," meaning building houses facing the Qiblah. Perhaps the meaning of this Ayah is that as long as Pharaoh's power remains and he intends to destroy you, you should establish your acts of worship in your homes. (344) Like the first three years of the Prophet of Islam's mission. The best glad tidings for believers who are in bondage is the glad tidings of the acceptance of their prayers, so that they may be saved from the oppression of the Pharaohites and their mischief.

344) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- All the programs of the prophets, even the method of housing construction, are based on divine revelations. «اوحینا... ان تبوّءا»

2- Housing is one of Allah's blessings. «اوحینا... ان تبوّءا»

3- Religious leaders must also pay attention to the material and welfare issues of the community. «تبوّءا لقومكما»

4- The residential area of the believers must be separate from that of the disbelievers (kuffar), so that it may pave the way for the dignity, power, and independence of the faithful group. «تبوّءا لقومكما بمصر بیوتا»

5- The history of urban planning is confirmed up to the time of Prophet Musa عليه السلام. «تبؤّا... بیوتاً»

6- Islamic architecture and urban planning must be compatible with ideological goals, and the direction of the Qiblah should not be forgotten. «واجعلوا بیوتكم قبلة»

7- We should build houses in such a way that performing religious rituals and establishing prayer (salat) within them is practical. «واجعلوا بیوتكم قبلة»

8- The house of ideological individuals must be a place of prayer (musalla). «بیوتكم قبلة»

9- Houses facing one another are more suitable in terms of concentration, protection, supervision, and mutual familiarity. «واجعلوا بیوتكم قبلة»

10- We must not allow strangers to infiltrate our neighborhood and society. «اجعلوا بیوتكم قبلة»

11- Prayer (salat) has been at the forefront of the programs of all prophets and a means of liberation. «اقیمواالصلوة»

Ayah

وَقَالَ مُوسَىٰ رَبَّنَآ إِنَّكَ ءَاتَيْتَ فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَأَهُۥ زِينَةً وَأَمْوَٰلًا فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا رَبَّنَا لِيُضِلُّوا۟ عَن سَبِيلِكَ ۖ رَبَّنَا ٱطْمِسْ عَلَىٰٓ أَمْوَٰلِهِمْ وَٱشْدُدْ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِهِمْ فَلَا يُؤْمِنُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ (۸۸)
Moses said, ‘Our Lord! You have given Pharaoh and his elite glamour and wealth in the life of this world, our Lord, that they may lead [people] astray from Your way! Our Lord! Blot out their wealth and harden their hearts so that they do not believe until they sight the painful punishment.’ (88)

Key Points

Cutting off oil pipelines, blocking trade and military routes, and burning the enemy's ammunition depots are examples of the meaning of «ربّنا اطمس على اموالهم» (Our Lord, obliterate their wealth).

Messages

  1. The word «ربّنا» (Our Lord) plays an important role in supplication. («ربّنا» is repeated three times in the Ayah.)
  2. Whatever anyone possesses is from Allah. «آتیت فرعون» (You have given Pharaoh).
  3. Wealth and possessions are not a sign of favor with Allah. «آتیت فرعون وملئه زینة...» (You have given Pharaoh and his chiefs adornment...).
  4. Disbelief does not prevent worldly success. «آتیت فرعون... زینة واموالا فى الحیاة الدنیا» (You have given Pharaoh... adornment and wealth in the life of this world).
  5. The material displays of the arrogant are only for the few days of this world. «فى الحیاة الدنیا» (in the life of this world).
  6. Tyrants (Taghuts) are themselves misguided and also lead others astray. «لیضلّوا» (so that they may lead astray).
  7. The curses of the prophets are not based on personal malice, but for the sake of preserving the faith and the community (Ummah). «لیضلّوا عن سبیلك» (so that they may lead astray from Your path).
  8. Capital and wealth in the hands of the unworthy cause deviation and negligence, just as poverty can be a ground for disbelief. «اموالاً... لیضلّوا عن سبیلك» (wealth... so that they may lead astray from Your path).
  9. In our supplications, let us not forget political prayers and curses against enemies. «ربّنا اطمس على اموالهم واشدد...» (Our Lord, obliterate their wealth and tighten...).
  10. Where reasoning and miracles are ineffective, it is the turn of the curse. «ربّنا اطمس...» (Our Lord, obliterate...).
  11. The hard-hearted will not believe the truth until they see the divine compulsion and wrath. «فلایؤمنوا حتّى یروا العذاب» (so they will not believe until they see the punishment).

Ayah

قَالَ قَدْ أُجِيبَت دَّعْوَتُكُمَا فَٱسْتَقِيمَا وَلَا تَتَّبِعَآنِّ سَبِيلَ ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْلَمُونَ (۸۹)
Said He, ‘Your supplication has already been granted. So be steadfast, and do not follow the way of those who do not know.’ (89)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, there was the curse (imprecation) of Moses (Musa) عليه السلام, and in this Ayah, there is the acceptance (answering) of the prayer (du'a) of Moses and Aaron (Harun) علیهما السلام. It is mentioned in narrations (Riwāyāt): Moses would supplicate, and Aaron would say Āmīn. The evidence for this is the word "ربّنا" (Rabbana - Our Lord). [345] Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: The interval between the imprecation of Moses and the drowning of Pharaoh was forty years. [346]

345) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 346) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

1- The supplication of the Prophets is answered. «قد اجیبت دعوتكما»

2- The answering of Moses' prayer is a sign of the validity and correctness of his requests regarding the destruction of the Pharaoh's people. «اجیبت دعوتكما»

3- Even after the acceptance of the prayer, patience and perseverance are necessary. «فاستقیما»

4- Divine leaders must be firm and decisive in the face of ignorant ideas, because weakness is a sign of ignorance. «فاستقیما ولاتتّبعان سبیل الذین لایعلمون»

5- Following the people of knowledge (Ahl al-'Ilm) is permissible. «لاتتّبعان سبیل الذین لایعلمون»

Ayah

۞ وَجَـٰوَزْنَا بِبَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ ٱلْبَحْرَ فَأَتْبَعَهُمْ فِرْعَوْنُ وَجُنُودُهُۥ بَغْيًا وَعَدْوًا ۖ حَتَّىٰٓ إِذَآ أَدْرَكَهُ ٱلْغَرَقُ قَالَ ءَامَنتُ أَنَّهُۥ لَآ إِلَـٰهَ إِلَّا ٱلَّذِىٓ ءَامَنَتْ بِهِۦ بَنُوٓا۟ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ وَأَنَا۠ مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ (۹٠)
We carried the Children of Israel across the sea, whereat Pharaoh and his troops pursued them, out of defiance and aggression. When overtaken by drowning, he called out, ‘I believe that there is no god except Him in whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am one of those who submit [to Him]!’ (90)

Key Points

The meaning of al-bahr (the sea) in this Ayah is the Nile River, which resembles a sea. Crossing it was by divine command, when He commanded Moses (peace be upon him) to strike the Nile River with his staff; the water parted, the seabed dried, and Moses and his companions crossed over it. Repenting and believing at the time of death is ineffective. As He says: «ولیست التوبة للّذین یعملون السیّئات حتّى اذا حضر احدهم الموت قال انّى تبتُ الآن» [347] The repentance of wrongdoers who repent and express remorse on the verge of death is not accepted.

347) An-Nisa, 18.

Messages

1- In the struggle against tyrants, we must rely on Allah, for He does not abandon us. «جاوزنا...»

2- In the cosmic order, all matters are by the will of Allah. «جاوزنا, ادركه الغرق»

3- The prayers of the prophets are ultimately answered. «ادركه الغرق»

4- Pharaoh, who intended to uproot Moses, [348] was himself destroyed. «ادركه الغرق» The lamp that God lights, whoever blows upon it will burn his beard.

5- Believing at the moment the divine punishment descends has no effect. «آمنت» while in the state of «ادركه الغرق»

6- The arrogant will one day fall into helplessness and supplication. «قال آمنت»

7- Arrogant ones like Pharaoh become believers and Muslims at the moment of death, but what good is it then! «آمنت»

8- Bitter blows and events remove the veils of heedlessness. «آمنت»

9- Non-divine powers are nothing more than a spider's web and will one day surrender. «قال... انا من المسلمین»

348) «فاراد اَنْ یستفزّهم من الأرض فاغرقناه ومن معه جمیعاً» Al-Isra, 102.

Ayahs

ءَآلْـَٔـٰنَ وَقَدْ عَصَيْتَ قَبْلُ وَكُنتَ مِنَ ٱلْمُفْسِدِينَ (۹۱)
[He was told,] ‘What! Now? When you have been disobedient heretofore and were among the agents of corruption?! (91)
فَٱلْيَوْمَ نُنَجِّيكَ بِبَدَنِكَ لِتَكُونَ لِمَنْ خَلْفَكَ ءَايَةً ۚ وَإِنَّ كَثِيرًا مِّنَ ٱلنَّاسِ عَنْ ءَايَـٰتِنَا لَغَـٰفِلُونَ (۹۲)
So today We shall deliver your body so that you may be a sign for those who come after you.’ Indeed many of the people are oblivious to Our signs. (92)

Key Points

A narration is cited in Tafsir Safi from Imam Reza عليه السلام, who said: Pharaoh was covered head to toe in armor and weapons, and according to the rule, he should have sunk to the bottom of the sea after drowning. However, the waves cast that heavy body onto a high shore. This itself was a miracle.

Messages

1- Repentance at the moment of death is useless; we must repent while there is still opportunity. «الآن» (Now).

2- The Quran informs about the future and the unseen. (Even now, after dozens of centuries, Pharaoh's mummified body is displayed in the Cairo Museum.) «ننجّیك ببدنك» (We shall save you with your body).

3- Lessons must be learned from the death and annihilation of the tyrants (Taghuts). «آیةً» (a sign).

4- Efforts must be made to preserve the signs and indications of Divine power and assistance. «لمن خلفك آیة» (for those who come after you as a sign).

5- Most people do not learn lessons from history and events. «كثیراً من الناس... غافلون» (Indeed, many among mankind... are heedless).

Ayah

وَلَقَدْ بَوَّأْنَا بَنِىٓ إِسْرَٰٓءِيلَ مُبَوَّأَ صِدْقٍ وَرَزَقْنَـٰهُم مِّنَ ٱلطَّيِّبَـٰتِ فَمَا ٱخْتَلَفُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ جَآءَهُمُ ٱلْعِلْمُ ۚ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ يَقْضِى بَيْنَهُمْ يَوْمَ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ فِيمَا كَانُوا۟ فِيهِ يَخْتَلِفُونَ (۹۳)
Certainly We settled the Children of Israel in a worthy settlement and We provided them with all the good things, and they did not differ until [after] the knowledge had come to them. Your Lord will indeed judge between them on the Day of Resurrection concerning that about which they used to differ. (93)

Key Points

The word ṣidq (truth/sincerity) in the culture of the Quran sometimes follows other words and conveys the meaning of worthy, good, and appropriate, such as: qadam ṣidq (a footing of truth) [349], mudkhal ṣidq (an entrance of truth), mukhraj ṣidq (an exit of truth) [350], lisān ṣidq (a tongue of truth) [351], wa‘d aṣ-ṣidq (the promise of truth) [352], maq‘ad ṣidq (a seat of truth) [353], and mubawwa’ ṣidq (a dwelling of truth). This means that ṣidq (truth) and conformity to reality is a value for everything. Here, too, it seems the intended meaning is the desirability of the dwelling place of the Children of Israel in terms of climate and blessings—namely, Syria (Sham) and Palestine. Makān ṣidq (a place of truth) means that which is truly the correct station, exactly as it ought to be. The dwelling place of the Children of Israel possessed all the conditions for life and was also very fertile, which is indicated by the phrase, «رزقناهم من الطیبات» (We provided them with good things).

349) Yunus, 2. 350) Al-Isra, 80. 351) Maryam, 50. 352) Al-Ahqaf, 16. 353) Al-Qamar, 55.

Messages

1- Having a suitable dwelling is one of Allah’s blessings and is favored by the divine religions. «مبوّء صدق»

2- In the movements of the prophets, besides spirituality, the material dimensions of people's lives are also given attention. «بوّأنا، رزقنا»

3- Although often after revolutions and transformations, the economic balance of society is disrupted, leading to inflation or famine, Allah saved the Children of Israel from shortages by overthrowing the Pharaonic regime. «رزقناهم»

4- Disunity neutralizes all of Allah’s blessings. «بوّأنا، رزقنا، اختلفوا»

5- The root of disagreements is not always material and welfare issues; sometimes it is whims and desires. «بوّأنا، رزقنا، اختلفوا»

6- Knowledge alone is not salvific. «اختلفوا، جائهم العلم»

7- The Quran directs its strongest criticism toward conscious disagreements. «جاءهم العلم»

Ayah

فَإِن كُنتَ فِى شَكٍّ مِّمَّآ أَنزَلْنَآ إِلَيْكَ فَسْـَٔلِ ٱلَّذِينَ يَقْرَءُونَ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ مِن قَبْلِكَ ۚ لَقَدْ جَآءَكَ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّكَ فَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُمْتَرِينَ (۹٤)
So if you are in doubt about what We have sent down to you, ask those who read the Book [revealed] before you. The truth has certainly come to you from your Lord; so do not be among the skeptics. (94)

Key Points

In a narration from Imam Hadi علیه السلام, we read: Ignorant people used to ask: Why does an angel not descend upon us instead of a Prophet? Allah answered them by revealing this Ayah and by stating that all Prophets had a human life. [354]

Question: Although previous heavenly books have been distorted, why are the scholars of the People of the Book made the reference and judge in this Ayah? Answer: Because even though parts of the previous heavenly books have been distorted, reliable individuals and statements can still be found among the scholars of the People of the Book. [355]

Quranic Question and Answer Question: How does the Quran state: «ان كنت فى شكٍّ» (If you are in doubt), O Prophet! If you doubt the truthfulness of the Quran, ask the People of the Book. Given that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله was never in doubt? And reason does not accept that a Prophet would doubt the revelation?

Answer: 1- The word in (اِن) (if) is hypothetical and does not indicate certainty or occurrence. For example, «ان كان للرحمن ولد» [356] (If the Most Gracious had a son). Even though the Ayah «لم یلد ولم یولد» explicitly negates having a son.

2- In Ayah 104 of this Surah, the Prophet addresses the people, saying: If you are in doubt about my religion. «ان كنتم فى شكٍّ من دینى». Therefore, the Prophet Himself was not in doubt, otherwise, He would not have issued a challenge (tahaddi). Furthermore, the Quran states: «آمن الرسول بما انزل الیه» [357] (The Messenger believes in what has been revealed to Him).

3- Sometimes the Quran addresses the Prophet, but the intended audience is others. Like this Ayah: «امّا یبلغنّ عندك الكبر احدهما اوكلاهما...» [358] (If one or both of them reach old age while with you). While the father of the Messenger passed away before His birth and His mother passed away when He was a child, and at the time of the revelation of this Ayah, the Prophet did not have parents who were old. The Ayah is an example of the idiom, "Speak to the door so the wall hears," which in Arabic is expressed as: «ایّاك اعنى و اسمعى یا جاره» (I mean you, but listen, O neighbor). In educational matters, sometimes the address is directed toward great figures and personalities so that others take heed of their own actions.

4- Sometimes an Ayah addresses the Prophet, but the Muslims are the intended audience, as the subsequent clause is also plural. For example: «یا ایها النبى، اذا طلقتم...» [359] (O Prophet, when you [plural] divorce...) and it does not say tallaqta (you [singular] divorce). Furthermore, we know that the Prophet never divorced a woman.

5- If, based on this Ayah, the revelation were to be questioned and doubted, then the truthfulness of this very Ayah, which says, "If you are in doubt," must also be doubted!!

6- Doubt and questions were raised for the People of the Book, and they were embarrassed to ask. Allah opened the way for them to ask through this statement.

7- It might be for the sake of accommodation and engagement with the opponent in the discussion, like when Prophet Abraham said to the star-worshippers: «هذا ربى» [360] (This [star] is my Lord).

8- When the Messenger of Allah صلى الله علیه وآله heard this Ayah, He said: I am not in doubt. Imam Sadiq علیه السلام also stated: By Allah, the Prophet was not in doubt. [361]

354) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 355) Tafsir Kabir by Fakhr al-Razi. 356) Maryam, 91. 357) Al-Baqarah, 285. 358) Al-Isra, 23. 359) Al-Talaq, 1. 360) Al-An'am, 76-78. 361) Tafsir Safi.

Messages

1- Eliminating doubt in beliefs is essential. «ان كنت فى شكّ»

2- When in doubt, one must refer to scholars. «فسئل الّذین یقرؤن الكتاب» (Thus, the word of a scholar is a proof.)

3- Heavenly books confirm one another. «یقرؤن الكتاب»

4- Some of the People of the Book speak the truth. «فسئل الّذین یقرؤن الكتاب»

5- The leader must be decisive and allow no hesitation to enter his heart. «فلاتكوننّ من الممترین»

6- Excellence is that which enemies testify to. After the opponents acknowledge its truthfulness, do you still doubt?! «فلاتكونن من الممترین»

Ayah

وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْخَـٰسِرِينَ (۹٥)
And do not be of those who deny the signs of Allah, [for] then you shall be among the losers. (95)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, [Allah] prohibited doubt concerning the Quran; here, He prohibits the denial of the verses (Ayat). It is possible that if doubt is not removed, it may eventually lead to denial. All the analyses and responses presented in the previous Ayah regarding "doubt" are also relevant here, because denial is worse than doubt. Many verses (Ayat) and narrations (Ahadith) report the certainty (Yaqin) of the Prophet. Therefore, the intent and the addressees of these prohibitions are the people, not the person of the Prophet Himself. How could the one whose certainty has led others to certainty Himself fall into doubt or denial?! Although the Prophet is infallible (Ma'sum), He has been addressed and warned repeatedly in the Quran so that the people may be warned through Him and take account of their own actions. Including: «لاتكوننّ من المشركین» (362), «لاتكوننّ ظهیراً للكافرین» (363), and «لایصدّنك عن آیات اللَّه بعد اذ انزلت الیك» (364).

362) An'am, 14. 363) Qasas, 86. 364) Qasas, 87.

Messages

1- Allah has warned His Prophet as well. «لاتكوننّ»

2- Losing the capital of faith and the leadership of the prophets is a loss. «الخاسرین»

Ayahs

إِنَّ ٱلَّذِينَ حَقَّتْ عَلَيْهِمْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۹٦)
Indeed those against whom your Lord’s judgement has become due will not have faith, (96)
وَلَوْ جَآءَتْهُمْ كُلُّ ءَايَةٍ حَتَّىٰ يَرَوُا۟ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَلِيمَ (۹۷)
even though every sign were to come to them, until they sight the painful punishment. (97)

Key Points

| Considering the phrase «حتّى یرد العذاب» (until the punishment comes) in Ayah 97 and the phrase «كشفنا عنهم العذاب» (We removed the punishment from them) in Ayah 98, what is intended by «كلمة ربّك» (the word of your Lord) is the Divine wrath.

Messages

1- Divine wrath is the due right of the disbelievers. [365] «حقت علیهم»

2- Being deprived of faith due to the continuous denial and obstinacy of the disbelievers is a requirement of Lordship and one of the Divine traditions. «حقّت علیهم كلمة ربك»

3- We should not expect all people to believe. «لایؤمنون و لو جائتهم كلّ آیة»

4- When the arrogant see the Divine wrath and punishment with their own eyes, they will believe out of fear and anxiety, but what good is that? «لایؤمنون... حتى یروا العذاب»

365) In another Ayah, we read: «لِیُنذِرَ من كانَ حیّاً و یحقّ القول على الكافرین» (That he may warn those who are alive and that the word may be justly decreed against the disbelievers.) [Yā Sīn, 7].

Ayah

فَلَوْلَا كَانَتْ قَرْيَةٌ ءَامَنَتْ فَنَفَعَهَآ إِيمَـٰنُهَآ إِلَّا قَوْمَ يُونُسَ لَمَّآ ءَامَنُوا۟ كَشَفْنَا عَنْهُمْ عَذَابَ ٱلْخِزْىِ فِى ٱلْحَيَوٰةِ ٱلدُّنْيَا وَمَتَّعْنَـٰهُمْ إِلَىٰ حِينٍ (۹۸)
Why has there not been any town that might believe, so that its belief might benefit it, except the people of Jonah? When they believed, We removed from them the punishment of disgrace in the life of this world, and We provided for them for a while. (98)

Key Points

Although the history of Prophet Noah and Moses is detailed in this Surah, the story of the repentance of the people of Jonah is mentioned briefly, in half an Ayah, yet the name given to this Surah is Surah Yunus (Jonah). Perhaps this is due to the sensitivity and importance of the action taken by the people of Jonah, who repented in the final moments, and Allah accepted their repentance. Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: Prophet Yunus عليه السلام preached from the age of thirty until sixty-three, and only two people believed in him. The Prophet cursed the people and departed from among them. One of those two believers, who was a wise sage, upon seeing the Prophet's curse and his departure from the area, went to a high place and warned the people with a shout. The people were affected, and guided by him, they left the city, separated themselves from their children, and cried out and repented to Allah until they were forgiven. When Yunus عليه السلام returned, he saw that the people had not been destroyed. He asked the reason, and they explained the incident to him. [366]

366) Tafasir Majma' al-Bayan and Safi.

Messages

1- Man can save himself even at the edge of the abyss. «الاّ قوم یونس»

2- Timely faith and repentance remove divine punishment. «لمّا امنوا كشفنا» (Among all the past denying nations, only the people of Yunus repented and believed in time.)

3- The destiny of people is in their own hands. «آمنوا كشفنا»

4- The secret to human happiness, even in this world, is faith. «آمنوا ... متعنا»

5- Supplication and prayer both remove calamity and bring prosperity. (Considering the occasion of revelation) «كشفنا، متّعنا»

Ayah

وَلَوْ شَآءَ رَبُّكَ لَـَٔامَنَ مَن فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ كُلُّهُمْ جَمِيعًا ۚ أَفَأَنتَ تُكْرِهُ ٱلنَّاسَ حَتَّىٰ يَكُونُوا۟ مُؤْمِنِينَ (۹۹)
Had your Lord wished, all those who are on earth would have believed. Would you then force people until they become faithful? (99)

Key Points

Allah possesses both power and wisdom, and He uses His power in a manner and place that does not contradict wisdom. Since His wisdom necessitates that people be free, He never compels them to forced belief through His overwhelming power. However, the polytheists used to say: «لو شاء اللّه ما اشركنا» (367) If God had willed, we would not have associated partners (with Him). Therefore, our polytheism was God's will, and we became polytheists by compulsion! This Ayah can be a response to their statement: If Allah's will is based on compulsion, why would He compel deviation and polytheism, and not at least compel guidance and faith!?

367) Al-An'am, 148.

Messages

1- Faith is based on choice, not compulsion and imposition. «أفانت تكره»

2- The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله insisted on people believing out of compassion. «افأنت تكره الناس حتى یكونوا مؤمنین»

Ayah

وَمَا كَانَ لِنَفْسٍ أَن تُؤْمِنَ إِلَّا بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَيَجْعَلُ ٱلرِّجْسَ عَلَى ٱلَّذِينَ لَا يَعْقِلُونَ (۱٠٠)
No soul may have faith except by Allah’s leave, and He lays defilement on those who do not exercise their reason. (100)

Messages

1- People are neither compelled to believe (according to the previous Ayah) nor can they succeed in believing without divine guidance and grace. [368] «الاّ باذن اللّه»

2- Disbelief (Kufr) is impurity. «رجس»

3- Divine favor and the grace of faith encompass those who possess wisdom and intellect, while he who voluntarily refuses to think and reason becomes subject to Allah's wrath. «ویجعل الّرجس على الذین لایعقلون»

4- Sound intellect prepares the ground for faith, and lack of faith is a sign of lack of wisdom. «...لایعقلون»

368) لاجبر و لاتفویض بل امر بین الامرین» Bihar, Vol. 4, p. 197.

Ayah

قُلِ ٱنظُرُوا۟ مَاذَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَٱلْأَرْضِ ۚ وَمَا تُغْنِى ٱلْـَٔايَـٰتُ وَٱلنُّذُرُ عَن قَوْمٍ لَّا يُؤْمِنُونَ (۱٠۱)
Say, ‘Observe what is in the heavens and the earth.’ But neither signs nor warnings avail a people who have no faith. (101)

Key Points

In the previous Ayah, the lack of intellect (or reasoning) was considered a reason for disbelief (kufr). In this Ayah, reflection and contemplation are known as the path to faith (Iman). Nudhur (نُذُر) is either the plural of Nadhir (نَذیر), meaning the prophets, or the plural of Indhar (اِنذار), meaning warnings.

Messages

  1. Studying and contemplating creation is the best, simplest, and most universal way to know Allah. «انظروا...»

  2. An observation is valuable and effective in decision-making only when it is done by human will, not merely the act of seeing and the reflection of objects in the brain and nerves. «انظروا...»

  3. Divine signs (Ayat) and warnings are effective only for pure-natured and non-obstinate people. «وما تغنى الایات...»

  4. Man is free in choosing his school of thought (or path); therefore, he sometimes stands against the prophets, the heavenly books, and the signs of Allah in the world, and does not believe (have Iman). «قوم لایؤمنون»

Ayah

فَهَلْ يَنتَظِرُونَ إِلَّا مِثْلَ أَيَّامِ ٱلَّذِينَ خَلَوْا۟ مِن قَبْلِهِمْ ۚ قُلْ فَٱنتَظِرُوٓا۟ إِنِّى مَعَكُم مِّنَ ٱلْمُنتَظِرِينَ (۱٠۲)
Do they await anything except the like of the days of those who passed away before them? Say, ‘Then wait! I too am waiting along with you.’ (102)

Messages

1- The tradition and law of Allah are uniform regarding all people. «مثل ایام الذین خَلَوا من قبل»

2- The history of the predecessors is a lesson for the successors. «خَلَوا من قبل»

3- Let us not be deceived by the divine respites. «فانتظروا»

4- The end of irreligion is ruin. «فانتظروا»

5- The obstinacy of the people sometimes made even the prophets despair, to the extent that they would declare their despair. «قل فانتظروا انى معكم من المنتظرین»

Ayah

ثُمَّ نُنَجِّى رُسُلَنَا وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ حَقًّا عَلَيْنَا نُنجِ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱٠۳)
Then We shall deliver Our apostles and those who have faith. Thus, it is a must for Us to deliver the faithful. (103)

Key Points

| Question: We have observed through experience that sometimes the good and the bad (the dry and the wet) burn together. Furthermore, Ayah 25 of Surah Al-Anfal warns us to fear a trial (fitnah) that will not only afflict the wrongdoers but will also reach the believers. Therefore, how does this Ayah state that We save the believers when calamity descends? Answer: If the people of faith remain silent among the disbelievers and criminals, Allah's wrath encompasses everyone. However, if they fulfill their divine duty, which is enjoining the right and forbidding the wrong (Nahy 'anil-Munkar), Allah saves them when danger arises.

Messages

1- Allah does not afflict the Prophets and the believers with His wrath. «ننجّى رسلنا... ننج المؤمنین»

2- It is among the divine traditions and among the rights that the believers have upon Allah that He saves and supports them during punishment and danger. «كذلك حقّاً علینا ننج المؤمنین»

3- The promise of salvation and deliverance is the best support for divine movements. «حقّاًعلینا ننج المؤمنین»

4- The future belongs to the believers. «حقّاً علینا ننج المؤمنین»

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلنَّاسُ إِن كُنتُمْ فِى شَكٍّ مِّن دِينِى فَلَآ أَعْبُدُ ٱلَّذِينَ تَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ وَلَـٰكِنْ أَعْبُدُ ٱللَّهَ ٱلَّذِى يَتَوَفَّىٰكُمْ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُؤْمِنِينَ (۱٠٤)
Say, ‘O people! if you are in doubt about my religion, then [know that] I do not worship those whom you worship besides Allah. Rather, I worship only Allah, who causes you to die, and I have been commanded to be among the faithful, (104)

Messages

1- The doubt of others should not cause us to doubt or hesitate. Decisiveness is a condition of leadership. «ان كنتم فى شكّ من دینى فلا اعبد...»

2- Any form of compromise, flattery, or flexibility with the disbelievers is forbidden; we must make the front of disbelief despair of us. «فلا اعبد...»

3- The enemy is covetous even of the leaders of Islam. Therefore, we must explicitly declare our disavowal and refusal to surrender. «فلا اعبد»

4- The One in Whose Hand is death and life is worthy of worship. «اعبد الذى یتوفّاكم»

5- Although both death and life are solely in the Hand of Allah, this Ayah mentions death. This is because death is the best means of warning. «الذى یتوفّاكم»

6- The polytheists considered their deities to be rational beings. («الذین» is used for rational beings)

Ayahs

وَأَنْ أَقِمْ وَجْهَكَ لِلدِّينِ حَنِيفًا وَلَا تَكُونَنَّ مِنَ ٱلْمُشْرِكِينَ (۱٠٥)
and that: ‘‘Dedicate yourself to the religion, as a Hanif, and never be one of the polytheists. (105)
وَلَا تَدْعُ مِن دُونِ ٱللَّهِ مَا لَا يَنفَعُكَ وَلَا يَضُرُّكَ ۖ فَإِن فَعَلْتَ فَإِنَّكَ إِذًا مِّنَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۱٠٦)
